prov.heading: Effect of opting in by employees

para.text: An employee who opts in under section 34(1)﻿(a) or (b)—
is liable in accordance with subpart 1 of Part 3 to deduction of contributions—
from the salary or wages paid by the employee’s employer; and
from the salary or wages paid in respect of any other new employment that the employee starts after opting in; and
must become a member of a KiwiSaver scheme under subpart 2 of this Part.

If an employee to whom subsection (1)﻿(a)﻿(i) applies has more than 1 employer who pays salary or wages to them, then, despite subsection (1)﻿(a)﻿(i), they may choose 1 or more employers who must make deductions of contributions from salary or wages in accordance with subpart 1 of Part 3.
The employee must continue to be a member of a KiwiSaver scheme until the earliest of—
the date the employee may withdraw an amount from the scheme under clause 4 of the KiwiSaver scheme rules (which relates to lock-in of funds); or
the provider terminating the employee’s membership of a KiwiSaver scheme under clause 4(3) of the KiwiSaver scheme rules (which relates to zero account balances); or
the date of withdrawal or transfer to a foreign scheme in the case of permanent emigration under clause 14 of the KiwiSaver scheme rules.
The employee continues to be liable for automatic deduction of contributions in accordance with subsection (1)﻿(a) in respect of salary or wages until the earliest of—
the dates referred to in subsection (2); or
the date on which section 62 (other than section 62(a)) otherwise applies to that payment of salary or wages.

history.history-note: Section 36(1): amended, on 19 December 2007, by section 39(a) of the Taxation (KiwiSaver) Act 2007 (2007 No 110).
Section 36(1)﻿(a): amended, on 19 December 2007, by section 39(b)﻿(i) of the Taxation (KiwiSaver) Act 2007 (2007 No 110).
Section 36(1)﻿(a): amended, on 19 December 2007, by section 39(b)﻿(ii) of the Taxation (KiwiSaver) Act 2007 (2007 No 110).
Section 36(1)﻿(a)﻿(i): amended, on 19 December 2007, by section 39(b)﻿(iii) of the Taxation (KiwiSaver) Act 2007 (2007 No 110).
Section 36(1B): inserted, on 19 December 2007, by section 39(c) of the Taxation (KiwiSaver) Act 2007 (2007 No 110).
Section 36(2)﻿(a): amended, on 1 July 2019, by section 311(1) of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2018–19, Modernising Tax Administration, and Remedial Matters) Act 2019 (2019 No 5).
Section 36(2)﻿(b): amended, on 1 July 2019, by section 311(2) of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2018–19, Modernising Tax Administration, and Remedial Matters) Act 2019 (2019 No 5).

prov.heading: Effect of opting in by persons other than employees

para.text: A person other than an employee who opts in must continue to be a member of a KiwiSaver scheme until the earliest of the events in section 36(2).

subpart.crosshead: Information about people who contract directly with providers

prov.heading: Providers must give notice to Commissioner if they contract directly with members

para.text: Every provider who contracts directly with a person (A) for membership of its KiwiSaver scheme must give notice to the Commissioner of that fact as soon as practicable after entering into the contract.
The notice must include all of the following information:
A’s name and address; and
A’s tax file number; and
the date of the first contribution received by the provider in respect of A (if any); and
if A is an employee, the contribution rate chosen by A under section 64(2); and
the name and address and tax file number of both the provider and the scheme; and
any other information that the Commissioner requires.

history.history-note: Section 38(2)﻿(d): replaced, on 1 April 2022, by section 10(2) of the Taxation (KiwiSaver, Student Loans, and Remedial Matters) Act 2020 (2020 No 5).

prov.heading: Commissioner must give notice to employer if provider gives notice that employee has opted in under section 38

para.text: The Commissioner must, as soon as practicable after receiving a notice under section 38 in respect of an employee who has opted in, give notice to each of the person’s employers, stating—

that the employer must start to make deductions of contributions from each payment of the person’s salary or wages that is calculated by the employer after the date on which the employer receives the notice under this section; and
the contribution rate chosen by the person under section 64(2); and
the person’s name and tax file number.

history.history-note: Section 39: amended, on 1 April 2020, by section 11(1) of the Taxation (KiwiSaver, Student Loans, and Remedial Matters) Act 2020 (2020 No 5).
Section 39(b): replaced, on 1 April 2022, by section 11(2) of the Taxation (KiwiSaver, Student Loans, and Remedial Matters) Act 2020 (2020 No 5).

subpart.crosshead: Information about overall KiwiSaver scheme that must be provided

prov.heading: Commissioner must supply information pack

para.text: The Commissioner must initially supply to each employer the number of information packs that the Commissioner reasonably believes will be a sufficient number to enable the employer to meet the employer’s obligations to supply information packs under this Act.
The Commissioner must also supply 1 or more information packs, on any reasonable request, to any person who so requests.

history.history-note: Section 40(1): amended (with effect on 1 July 2007), on 19 December 2007, by section 40(1) of the Taxation (KiwiSaver) Act 2007 (2007 No 110).
Section 40(2): amended (with effect on 1 July 2007), on 19 December 2007, by section 40(2) of the Taxation (KiwiSaver) Act 2007 (2007 No 110).

prov.heading: What information pack must contain

para.text: Every information pack supplied by the Commissioner under this subpart must contain—
a description of the overall KiwiSaver scheme; and
a statement that membership of the overall KiwiSaver scheme, and of any individual KiwiSaver scheme, is at the member’s own risk; and
a summary of what could happen under the default allocation rules or if an employer has a chosen KiwiSaver scheme; and
a description of how to access information about KiwiSaver schemes; and
a statement that people should seek financial advice from a financial advice provider (rather than an employer) if they want information in relation to—
their personal financial circumstances; or
deciding whether to opt in or opt out or not; or
choosing a KiwiSaver scheme or investment product of a KiwiSaver scheme; or
the overall KiwiSaver scheme or its financial concepts; and
an opt-out notice form; and
a statement about collection of personal information that complies with information privacy principle 3 set out in section 22 of the Privacy Act 2020; and
any other prescribed information.

history.history-note: Section 41(e): amended, on 15 March 2021, by section 98 of the Financial Services Legislation Amendment Act 2019 (2019 No 8).
Section 41(g): replaced, on 1 December 2020, by section 217 of the Privacy Act 2020 (2020 No 31).

prov.heading: Employer must supply information pack to certain employees

para.text: Every employer must supply an information pack to—
each employee who starts new employment with the employer and to whom the automatic enrolment rules apply, within 7 days of the employee starting the new employment; and
each employee who opts in under section 34(1)﻿(b), within 7 days of the employee notifying his or her employer of a change in their KiwiSaver status; and
each employee who requests an information pack in contemplation of opting in.
An employer is not liable for a penalty for a failure to supply an information pack under this section if the employer proves that—

the failure of the employer to supply the information pack was caused by the fact that the Commissioner had not given the employer enough information packs to enable the employer to meet its obligations under this Act; and
the employer notified the Commissioner that the employer needed more information packs, as soon as practicable after realising that the employer did not have enough.

history.history-note: Section 42(1)﻿(b): amended, on 1 April 2019, by section 379 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2017–18, Employment and Investment Income, and Remedial Matters) Act 2018 (2018 No 5).

prov.heading: Employer must also supply product disclosure statement for employer’s chosen KiwiSaver scheme (if any)

para.text: Every employer who supplies an information pack under section 42 must also, if an employer’s choice of KiwiSaver scheme is effective under section 47, supply to the employee at the same time—
the product disclosure statement for the offer of interests in that scheme; and

a statement that, if the employee does not choose his or her own KiwiSaver scheme, the employee will be allocated to the employer’s chosen KiwiSaver scheme (and not to one of the default KiwiSaver schemes by the Commissioner).

history.history-note: Section 43 heading: amended, on 1 December 2014, by section 81(1) of the Financial Markets (Repeals and Amendments) Act 2013 (2013 No 70).
Section 43(a): amended, on 1 December 2014, by section 81(2) of the Financial Markets (Repeals and Amendments) Act 2013 (2013 No 70).

subpart.heading: Allocation of people to KiwiSaver schemes

prov.heading: Outline of how people are allocated to KiwiSaver schemes under subpart

para.text: This subpart provides for people to be allocated to KiwiSaver schemes—
by the person choosing his or her own KiwiSaver scheme; or
if the person does not so choose, but if his or her employer has a chosen KiwiSaver scheme, by the person being allocated to the employer’s chosen KiwiSaver scheme; or
in any other case, by the person being allocated to a default KiwiSaver scheme nominated by the Commissioner.

subpart.crosshead: People may choose their own KiwiSaver scheme

prov.heading: People may choose their own KiwiSaver scheme

para.text: A person may, at any time, choose the KiwiSaver scheme of which he or she will be a member by contracting directly with the provider of the scheme to become a member of that scheme.

prov.heading: Employer may choose scheme for employees

para.text: An employer may, at any time, choose a KiwiSaver scheme of which the employer’s employees will become members if the employees do not choose their own KiwiSaver scheme.

However, an employer may choose a KiwiSaver scheme under this section only if all permanent employees of the employer are eligible to be members of the scheme (to the extent that this Act applies to the employees).
In this section, permanent employees means employees—
who are not employed in temporary employment (as described in section 12); and
to whom the automatic enrolment rules apply, or would apply but for the application of section 14.

history.history-note: Section 46(2): amended, on 19 December 2007, by section 41(1) of the Taxation (KiwiSaver) Act 2007 (2007 No 110).
Section 46(3): substituted, on 21 May 2007, by section 62 of the Taxation (KiwiSaver and Company Tax Rate Amendments) Act 2007 (2007 No 19).
Section 46(3)﻿(b): amended, on 19 December 2007, by section 41(2) of the Taxation (KiwiSaver) Act 2007 (2007 No 110).

prov.heading: When employer choice of KiwiSaver scheme is effective

para.text: The method by which an employer may choose a KiwiSaver scheme is—
by agreeing with the provider that the provider will provide access to the scheme for the employer’s employees; and
by giving notice to the Commissioner of—
the name, address, and tax file number of the employer; and
the name, address, and tax file number of both the provider and the chosen KiwiSaver scheme.
The employer’s choice of KiwiSaver scheme is effective—
as from the date on which the notice in subsection (1)﻿(b) is accepted by the Commissioner, or on any later date specified in the notice; and
until the effective date of the earliest of any of the following notices:
notice given by the employer to the Commissioner of an alternative choice of scheme under subsection (1); or
notice given by the employer to the Commissioner stating that the employer no longer has a chosen KiwiSaver scheme; or

notice given by the Commissioner to the employer stating that the employer’s choice of KiwiSaver scheme has been revoked by the Commissioner on the grounds that the Commissioner is not satisfied that the scheme is eligible to be the employer’s chosen scheme under section 46.

prov.heading: Effect of employer choice of KiwiSaver scheme

para.text: This section applies when—
an employer’s choice of KiwiSaver scheme is effective under section 47; and
an employee of the employer has not directly contracted to be a member of a KiwiSaver scheme with the provider of a scheme; and
the employee is an employee—
to whom the automatic enrolment rules apply; or
who opted in under section 34(1)﻿(b); and
more than 62 days have passed since the Commissioner received the first contribution in respect of the employee; and
there is no relevant dispute under section 212 or 213 in relation to Part 2 or 3.
On the first day that this section applies to an employee, the employee is treated, for the purposes of this Act and the Financial Markets Conduct Act 2013, as having—
been given a product disclosure statement for the offer of interests in the employer’s chosen KiwiSaver scheme; and
immediately after, applied to become a member of that scheme; and
become a member of that scheme.
The provider of the employer’s chosen KiwiSaver scheme must accept that application and register the employee as a member of the scheme.
The membership contract of the KiwiSaver scheme is binding on the employee and the provider, and enforceable as if it were a contract that was freely and voluntarily entered into.
The contract may be varied, replaced, cancelled, or otherwise terminated, and the managed investment products may be returned,—
to the extent permitted by section 131; and
except as provided by that section, in the same way as if the contract were freely and voluntarily entered into.

The Commissioner must, as soon as practicable, give notice to the provider of the scheme of the employee’s name, address, date of birth (if known to the Commissioner), tax file number, and any other personal information that the Commissioner considers relevant.

history.history-note: Section 48(1): substituted (with effect on 1 July 2007), on 19 December 2007, by section 42 of the Taxation (KiwiSaver) Act 2007 (2007 No 110).
Section 48(1)﻿(d): amended, on 1 April 2020, by section 12 of the Taxation (KiwiSaver, Student Loans, and Remedial Matters) Act 2020 (2020 No 5).
Section 48(2): replaced, on 1 December 2014, by section 82 of the Financial Markets (Repeals and Amendments) Act 2013 (2013 No 70).
Section 48(3): replaced, on 1 December 2014, by section 82 of the Financial Markets (Repeals and Amendments) Act 2013 (2013 No 70).
Section 48(4): replaced, on 1 December 2014, by section 82 of the Financial Markets (Repeals and Amendments) Act 2013 (2013 No 70).
Section 48(5): replaced, on 1 December 2014, by section 82 of the Financial Markets (Repeals and Amendments) Act 2013 (2013 No 70).
Section 48(6): repealed, on 1 December 2014, by section 82 of the Financial Markets (Repeals and Amendments) Act 2013 (2013 No 70).

prov.heading: Effect on existing members of change, etc, in employer’s chosen KiwiSaver scheme

para.text: A notice under section 47(2)﻿(b) does not affect any person who became a member of a KiwiSaver scheme while the scheme was the employer’s chosen KiwiSaver scheme.
However, subsection (1) does not limit section 181 of the Financial Markets Conduct Act 2013 (which provides for transfers with FMA consent in certain circumstances).

history.history-note: Section 49(2): amended, on 1 December 2014, by section 83 of the Financial Markets (Repeals and Amendments) Act 2013 (2013 No 70).

prov.heading: Commissioner provisionally allocates certain people to default KiwiSaver schemes and sends product disclosure statement

para.text: This section applies, in respect of a person who is an employee of an employer and their employment with that employer, when the Commissioner has received from the employer,—
notice under section 23 of the person’s automatic enrolment; or
notice under section 34(3) of a person’s opt-in under section 34(1)﻿(b).
However, this section does not apply to a person referred to in subsection (1)﻿(a) or (b)—
who is an employee of an employer whose chosen KiwiSaver scheme is effective under section 47; or
who has opted out; or
if the Commissioner has been notified by a provider that the person has become a member of a KiwiSaver scheme.

text.insertwords: As soon as practicable, the Commissioner must, in respect of the person’s employment with the employer,—

para.text: provisionally allocate, on a sequential basis, the person to a default investment product of a default KiwiSaver scheme that is nominated by the Commissioner; and

give notice to the person of that allocation, including the name and address of the provider of the nominated default KiwiSaver scheme, and of the name of the default investment product of that scheme, to which the person has been provisionally allocated; and
send to the person the product disclosure statement relating to that product in that scheme; and
give notice to the person of what will happen if the person does not choose his or her own KiwiSaver scheme.
Subsection (3) also applies, with necessary modifications, and as provided in section 57 in cases to which that section applies, to a person when—
the Commissioner receives notice under section 58 of the person having ceased to be eligible to be a member of his or her employer’s chosen KiwiSaver scheme; or

the Commissioner receives notice under section 212(1)﻿(b) of the Financial Markets Conduct Act 2013 (which relates to transfers to another scheme on a scheme’s winding up) and paragraph (ba) does not apply; or
a scheme winds up, if that winding up is after the Commissioner receives notice under that section; or
the Commissioner receives notice under section 131(1); or

the Commissioner receives notice of any other situation where a person is not, or is no longer, eligible to become or be a member of a certain KiwiSaver scheme and needs to be allocated to a KiwiSaver scheme under this section in order to comply with this Act.

history.history-note: Section 50 heading: amended, on 1 December 2014, by section 84(1) of the Financial Markets (Repeals and Amendments) Act 2013 (2013 No 70).
Section 50(1): substituted (with effect on 1 July 2007), on 19 December 2007, by section 43(1) of the Taxation (KiwiSaver) Act 2007 (2007 No 110).
Section 50(3): amended (with effect on 1 July 2007), on 19 December 2007, by section 43(2) of the Taxation (KiwiSaver) Act 2007 (2007 No 110).
Section 50(3)﻿(c): amended, on 1 December 2014, by section 84(2) of the Financial Markets (Repeals and Amendments) Act 2013 (2013 No 70).
Section 50(4): amended, on 21 December 2010, by section 173(1) of the Taxation (GST and Remedial Matters) Act 2010 (2010 No 130).
Section 50(4)﻿(a): amended, on 21 December 2010, by section 173(2) of the Taxation (GST and Remedial Matters) Act 2010 (2010 No 130).
Section 50(4)﻿(b): replaced, on 1 December 2014, by section 84(3) of the Financial Markets (Repeals and Amendments) Act 2013 (2013 No 70).
Section 50(4)﻿(ba): inserted, on 1 December 2014, by section 84(3) of the Financial Markets (Repeals and Amendments) Act 2013 (2013 No 70).
Section 50(4)﻿(bb): repealed, on 1 December 2014, by section 84(3) of the Financial Markets (Repeals and Amendments) Act 2013 (2013 No 70).
Section 50(4)﻿(c): replaced, on 1 December 2014, by section 84(3) of the Financial Markets (Repeals and Amendments) Act 2013 (2013 No 70).
Section 50(4)﻿(d): amended, on 21 December 2010, by section 173(5) of the Taxation (GST and Remedial Matters) Act 2010 (2010 No 130).

prov.heading: Completion of allocation to default KiwiSaver scheme if person does not choose his or her own KiwiSaver scheme

para.text: This section and section 52 apply, at the final allocation date specified in subsection (4), to a person (A) who has been provisionally allocated under section 50 if the Commissioner has not been notified by that date by a provider that A has applied to become a member of a KiwiSaver scheme.
Despite subsection (1), subsections (4) and (5) do not apply if section 50(4)﻿(b) or (ba) applies. Instead, the allocation under section 50(3) is treated as completed on the day on which it occurs.
The Commissioner must give notice to A that the allocation of A is now completed as per the provisional allocation.

The Commissioner must give notice to the provider of the default KiwiSaver scheme that A has been allocated to the scheme, and A’s name, address, date of birth (if known to the Commissioner), tax file number, and any other personal information that the Commissioner considers relevant.
The final allocation date is,—
in the case of a person referred to in section 50(1)﻿(a) or (b), as soon as practicable after the 62nd day after the Commissioner receives the first contribution in respect of A:
in the case of a person referred to in section 50(4)﻿(a), (c), or (d), 62 days after the date on which the Commissioner receives that notice.

However, if a dispute in relation to Part 2 or 3 is underway under section 212 or 213 as at the date which would otherwise be the final allocation date, the final allocation date is the effective date of the notice given by the Commissioner to the effect that the dispute has been resolved or has otherwise been terminated.

history.history-note: Section 51(1B): inserted, on 21 December 2010, by section 174(1) of the Taxation (GST and Remedial Matters) Act 2010 (2010 No 130).
Section 51(1B): amended (with effect on 1 December 2014), on 23 March 2020, by section 13(1) of the Taxation (KiwiSaver, Student Loans, and Remedial Matters) Act 2020 (2020 No 5).
Section 51(4)﻿(a): amended, on 1 April 2020, by section 13(2) of the Taxation (KiwiSaver, Student Loans, and Remedial Matters) Act 2020 (2020 No 5).
Section 51(4)﻿(a): amended (with effect on 1 July 2007), on 19 December 2007, by section 44(1) of the Taxation (KiwiSaver) Act 2007 (2007 No 110).
Section 51(4)﻿(b): amended, on 1 April 2020, by section 13(3) of the Taxation (KiwiSaver, Student Loans, and Remedial Matters) Act 2020 (2020 No 5).
Section 51(4)﻿(b): amended, on 21 December 2010, by section 174(2) of the Taxation (GST and Remedial Matters) Act 2010 (2010 No 130).
Section 51(5): amended (with effect on 1 July 2007), on 19 December 2007, by section 44(2) of the Taxation (KiwiSaver) Act 2007 (2007 No 110).

prov.heading: Effect of completion of allocation

para.text: A person whose allocation is completed under section 51 is treated, for the purposes of this Act and the Financial Markets Conduct Act 2013, as having—
been given a product disclosure statement for the offer of interests in the default KiwiSaver scheme; and
immediately after, applied to become a member of that scheme; and
become a member of that scheme.
The provider of the default KiwiSaver scheme to which the person has been allocated must accept that application and register the person as a member of the scheme.
The membership contract of the default KiwiSaver scheme is binding on the person and the provider, and enforceable as if it were a contract that was freely and voluntarily entered into.
The contract may be varied, replaced, cancelled, or otherwise terminated, and the managed investment products may be returned,—
to the extent permitted by section 131; and
except as provided in that section, in the same way as if the contract were freely and voluntarily entered into.

history.history-note: Section 52: replaced, on 1 December 2014, by section 85 of the Financial Markets (Repeals and Amendments) Act 2013 (2013 No 70).

prov.heading: Person may be member of only 1 KiwiSaver scheme at any one time

para.text: A person may be a member of only 1 KiwiSaver scheme at any one time.
This section does not limit subpart 3.
This section does not prevent people from having more than 1 account or investment product of any one KiwiSaver scheme.

subpart.heading: Transfers between KiwiSaver schemes and between complying superannuation fund and KiwiSaver scheme

history.history-note: Subpart 3 heading: amended, on 1 July 2007, by section 222 of the Taxation (Savings Investment and Miscellaneous Provisions) Act 2006 (2006 No 81).

prov.heading: Application

para.text: Sections 55 and 56 apply if a person wishes to transfer KiwiSaver schemes or to transfer from a complying superannuation fund to a KiwiSaver scheme (a voluntary transfer).

Sections 57 to 59 apply if a person has to transfer KiwiSaver schemes or to transfer from a complying superannuation fund to a KiwiSaver scheme (an involuntary transfer). An example of an involuntary transfer may be where a person ceases to be eligible to be a member of their current KiwiSaver scheme, or their complying superannuation fund.

history.history-note: Section 54: substituted, on 1 July 2007, by section 223 of the Taxation (Savings Investment and Miscellaneous Provisions) Act 2006 (2006 No 81).

prov.heading: People may transfer between KiwiSaver schemes and between complying superannuation fund and KiwiSaver scheme

para.text: A person may, at any time, transfer from a KiwiSaver scheme or a complying superannuation fund (an old scheme) to a new KiwiSaver scheme (a new scheme) by contracting directly with the provider of the new KiwiSaver scheme to become a member of that scheme.
The transfer is effective in relation to the Commissioner’s functions under this Act only when the Commissioner receives notice of the transfer under section 56(1).

history.history-note: Section 55 heading: amended, on 1 July 2007, by section 224(1) of the Taxation (Savings Investment and Miscellaneous Provisions) Act 2006 (2006 No 81).
Section 55(1): amended, on 1 July 2007, by section 224(2) of the Taxation (Savings Investment and Miscellaneous Provisions) Act 2006 (2006 No 81).

prov.heading: Notification of transfers and requirement to transfer funds and information

para.text: The provider of the new scheme must, as soon as practicable, give notice—
to the Commissioner—
that the person has transferred to the new scheme; and
of the person’s name, address, date of birth, and tax file number; and
of the new scheme’s name and tax file number; and
to the provider of the old scheme—
that the person has ceased to be a member of the old scheme and the effective date of the transfer; and
of the new scheme’s name and address; and
that the provider of the old scheme must transfer the funds and information required to be transferred in accordance with subsection (3).
The provider of the new scheme must also give evidence to the provider of the old scheme that the member wishes to transfer to the new scheme.
The provider of the old scheme that is given notice under subsection (1) must—
transfer the member’s accumulation to the new scheme; and
give notice to the member of the amount so transferred; and
give notice to the provider of the new scheme—
of the date on which the member first became a member of a KiwiSaver scheme, if the old scheme is a KiwiSaver scheme; and
as to whether the member has made a withdrawal for the purpose of the purchase of a first home under clause 8 of the KiwiSaver scheme rules; and
of any savings suspensions in force; and
as to whether the Crown contribution under section 226 is included in the member’s accumulation transferred to the new scheme; and

of any information held by a provider of the old scheme that would be relevant to a provider of the new scheme making a claim under section 68C of the Tax Administration Act 1994, including information as to the periods for which claims have already been made.
The provider must comply with subsection (3) within 10 working days of receiving that notice or any longer period agreed between the providers of the old and new schemes.

Despite subsections (3) and (4), in the circumstances described in subsection (6), the provider of the old scheme must not transfer the member’s accumulation to the new scheme other than by way of distributing the assets of the old scheme as the scheme is wound up.
The circumstances are that—
members have been notified of a proposal to transfer all of the members of the old scheme to another scheme under section 180 of the Financial Markets Conduct Act 2013; or
a copy of a winding-up order or resolution of the old scheme has been given to the FMA under section 212 of that Act.

history.history-note: Section 56(3)﻿(c)﻿(i): amended, on 1 July 2007, by section 225 of the Taxation (Savings Investment and Miscellaneous Provisions) Act 2006 (2006 No 81).

prov.heading: Involuntary transfers

para.text: This section applies if a person has to transfer KiwiSaver schemes or from a complying superannuation fund to a KiwiSaver scheme, including if—
the Commissioner has received a notice under section 58 that an employee has ceased to be eligible to be a member of an employer’s chosen scheme; or

the Commissioner receives notice under section 212(1)﻿(b) of the Financial Markets Conduct Act 2013 (which relates to transfers to another scheme on a scheme’s winding-up) and paragraph (ba) of this section does not apply; or
a scheme winds up, if that winding-up is after the Commissioner receives notice under section 212(1)﻿(b) of the Financial Markets Conduct Act 2013; or
the Commissioner receives notice under section 131(1); or
the Commissioner has received notice in accordance with clauses 4(a) and 5(a) in schedule 28 of the Income Tax Act 2007.
However, this section does not apply to a transfer to which section 181 of the Financial Markets Conduct Act 2013 applies.
The person must be allocated to a new scheme in accordance with the principles in section 44, but excluding section 44(b) (which relates to allocation to an employer’s chosen KiwiSaver scheme).

The Commissioner must take whatever steps the Commissioner thinks appropriate to ensure that, so far as practicable, the process for an involuntary transfer follows the process for a voluntary transfer under section 56.
Section 56(3) and (4) applies to an involuntary transfer, but as if the requirement in section 56(4) referred to 62 days instead of 10 working days.
Sections 45 to 53 apply to an involuntary transfer with necessary modifications.
Subsections (5) and (6) do not limit subsection (4).

Section 57(1)﻿(b): replaced, on 1 December 2014, by section 87(1) of the Financial Markets (Repeals and Amendments) Act 2013 (2013 No 70).

prov.heading: Information if employee ceases to be eligible to be member of employer’s chosen KiwiSaver scheme

para.text: The employer must give notice to the employee and the Commissioner if, under the terms of the employer’s chosen KiwiSaver scheme, the employee ceases to be eligible to be a member of that KiwiSaver scheme (for example, if the employee ceases to be an employee of the employer and the scheme applies only to the employer’s employees).

prov.heading: Commissioner must send information to involuntary transferees

para.text: The Commissioner must send to every person who is subject to an involuntary transfer under this subpart, as soon as practicable after the Commissioner receives the notice in respect of that person under section 57(1),—
an information pack, but not if section 57(1)﻿(b) applies; and
advice as to the effect of this subpart and of section 50.

history.history-note: Section 59(a): amended, on 21 December 2010, by section 176 of the Taxation (GST and Remedial Matters) Act 2010 (2010 No 130).

subpart.heading: Initial and confirmed back-dated validation of invalid membership

history.history-note: Subpart 4: added (with effect on 1 July 2007), on 19 December 2007, by section 47 of the Taxation (KiwiSaver) Act 2007 (2007 No 110).

prov.label: 59A
prov.heading: When this subpart applies

para.text: This subpart applies when, because of a mistake,—
this Act has been applied to a person who does not meet the requirements of section 6:
the automatic enrolment rules have been applied to a person who, at the time, does not meet the requirements of—
1 or more of section 10(a), (b), and (c)﻿(ii):
section 10(c)﻿(i):
the rule in section 33 allowing opt-in has been applied prior to 1 July 2019 to a person who, at the time, did not meet the requirements of section 33(a):
the rule in section 34 allowing opt-in has been applied to a person who, at the time, does not meet the requirements of section 10(c)﻿(i).

history.history-note: Section 59A: inserted (with effect on 1 July 2007), on 19 December 2007, by section 47 of the Taxation (KiwiSaver) Act 2007 (2007 No 110).
Section 59A(a): replaced, on 2 June 2016, by section 212 of the Taxation (Transformation: First Phase Simplification and Other Measures) Act 2016 (2016 No 27).
Section 59A(b): replaced, on 2 June 2016, by section 212 of the Taxation (Transformation: First Phase Simplification and Other Measures) Act 2016 (2016 No 27).
Section 59A(bb): inserted (with effect on 1 September 2021), on 30 March 2022, by section 247 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2021–22, GST, and Remedial Matters) Act 2022 (2022 No 10).
Section 59A(c): repealed, on 1 July 2019, by section 312 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2018–19, Modernising Tax Administration, and Remedial Matters) Act 2019 (2019 No 5 ).
Section 59A(d): inserted, on 2 June 2016, by section 212 of the Taxation (Transformation: First Phase Simplification and Other Measures) Act 2016 (2016 No 27).

prov.label: 59B
prov.heading: Initial back-dated validation

para.text: As soon as practicable after anyone discovers the mistake, they must notify the Commissioner or the relevant KiwiSaver scheme provider.

The person described in section 59A is treated as a person who meets the requirements of section 6, the requirements for the application of the automatic enrolment rules, the age requirement for the application of the opt-in rule in section 33, or the age requirement for the application of the opt-in rule in section 34, for a period—
starting on the earliest day on which this Act applies, the automatic enrolment rules, or the rule allowing opt-in were applied to the person because of the mistake described in section 59A; and
for a person referred to in section 59A(a), ending on the earlier of—
the day the mistake is discovered by the person’s KiwiSaver scheme provider:
the day the mistake is notified to the provider by the Commissioner or another person; and
for a person referred to in section 59A(b)﻿(i), (bb), and (d), ending on the earlier of—
the 92nd day after the mistake is discovered by the person’s KiwiSaver scheme provider:
the 92nd day after the mistake is notified to the provider by the Commissioner or another person:
the day the provider pays the amount of the member's accumulation, less the amount that was transferred from an Australian complying superannuation scheme for the person to the Commissioner; and
for a person referred to in section 59A(b)﻿(ii), ending on the earlier of—
the day the person reaches 19 years of age; and
the day the provider pays the amount of the member’s accumulation less the amount that was transferred from an Australian complying superannuation scheme for the person to the Commissioner.

prov.label: 59C
prov.heading: Confirmed back-dated validation

para.text: This section applies if,—

during the period of initial back-dated validation under section 59B, the person described in section 59A is a person to whom the automatic enrolment rules were applied because of the mistake described in section 59A(b)﻿(i), or the opt-in rule in section 33 was applied to a person because of the mistake described in section 59A(bb), and—
they do not opt out; and
the person described in section 59A is not a person to whom this Act has been applied because of a mistake described in section 59A(a).
The person is treated as a person—
in relation to whom no mistake described in section 59A(b)﻿(i) was made; and
who, at the time, met the requirements of the automatic enrolment rules described in section 59A(b)﻿(i); and
in relation to whom no mistake described in section 59A(bb) was made.
The Commissioner must notify the provider that this section applies.
The relevant provider does not pay the member’s accumulation for the person to the Commissioner.

history.history-note: Section 59C: inserted (with effect on 1 July 2007), on 19 December 2007, by section 47 of the Taxation (KiwiSaver) Act 2007 (2007 No 110).
Section 59C(1): replaced, on 1 April 2020, by section 17(1) of the Taxation (KiwiSaver, Student Loans, and Remedial Matters) Act 2020 (2020 No 5).

prov.label: 59CB
prov.heading: Confirmed back-dated validation for those under 18 years at time of enrolment or opt-in

para.text: This section applies when—
the automatic enrolment rules were applied to a person because of the mistake described in section 59A(b)﻿(ii):
the opt-in rule in section 34 was applied to a person because of the mistake described in section 59A(d).
This section does not apply to—
a person aged 16 years or under if all guardians of the person have contracted directly with a provider in the person’s name:
a person aged 16 years or over but less than 18 years if the person and a guardian of the person have contracted directly with a provider:
a person aged 18 years who—
has contracted directly with a provider:
has opted in under section 34:
has chosen an investment product in a default KiwiSaver scheme or transferred their funds under section 55 to another scheme.
The person may,—
if they are aged 15 years or under, opt out with consent from a guardian:
if they are aged 16 years or over but less than 19 years, opt out.
The person opts out under subsection (3) by notifying the Commissioner.
If the person opts out under subsection (3), the Commissioner must notify the provider and the person’s employer.

The person’s employer must stop making deductions from the person’s salary or wages, with effect on the next payment of salary or wages that the employer calculates after the date on which the employer receives the notice referred to in subsection (5).
The employer may refund any deduction to the person, rather than pay it to the Commissioner.
If the person does not opt out under subsection (3) before the day on which they reach the age of 19 years, the person is treated as a person—
in relation to whom no mistake described in section 59A(b)﻿(ii) or 59A(d) was made; and
who, at the time, met the requirements of the automatic enrolment rules or the age requirement for the application of the opt-in rule in section 34.
If the person does not opt out under subsection (3), the relevant provider does not pay the member’s accumulation for the person to the Commissioner.
References in this section to a guardian include an Oranga Tamariki guardian.

history.history-note: Section 59CB: inserted, on 2 June 2016, by section 215 of the Taxation (Transformation: First Phase Simplification and Other Measures) Act 2016 (2016 No 27).
Section 59CB(10): inserted, on 27 August 2019, by section 6 of the KiwiSaver (Oranga Tamariki Guardians) Amendment Act 2019 (2019 No 45).

prov.label: 59D
prov.heading: No confirmed backdated validation

para.text: This section applies when—
in relation to a person described in section 59A(a), the period described in section 59B(2)﻿(a) and (ab) has ended:

in relation to a person to whom the automatic enrolment rules were applied because of the mistake described in section 59A(b)﻿(i), or the opt-in rule in section 33 was applied to a person because of the mistake described in section 59A(bb), confirmed backdated validation under section 59C does not occur in the period of initial back-dated validation under section 59B:
in relation to a person described in section 59CB(1), the person opts out under section 59CB(3).
The relevant provider must immediately—
provide the Commissioner with a notice stating, for the relevant person,—
the amount of contributions received directly by the provider (not via the Commissioner), when they were received, and (if available) who they were paid by; and
the amounts paid out by the provider under a mortgage diversion facility, and when they were paid out; and

the amounts paid out by the provider to the person as permitted withdrawals, when they were paid out, the types of permitted withdrawals, and the amount of Crown contributions included in the permitted withdrawals; and

pay the amount of the member's accumulation, less the amount that was transferred from an Australian complying superannuation scheme, for the person to the Commissioner, if the provider has not already done so; and

pay the amount that was transferred from an Australian complying superannuation scheme (the transferor scheme) or the amount of the member's accumulation (whichever amount is smaller), for the person to—
the transferor scheme; or
an Australian complying superannuation scheme chosen by the person; or
an Australian complying superannuation scheme chosen by the Commissioner, if the person does not choose one and it is not appropriate to pay to the transferor scheme.
The Commissioner must pay, in accordance with subsection (4), as soon as practicable and without further authority than this section, an amount (the refund amount) equal to the total of—
the contributions received by the provider (whether directly or via the Commissioner), less the total of—
the amounts paid out by the provider under a mortgage diversion facility:
the amounts paid out by the provider to the person as permitted withdrawals, excluding the amount of Crown contributions included in the permitted withdrawals:
Crown contributions:
the amount that was transferred from an Australian complying superannuation scheme:
the contributions held in respect of the person in the holding account described in section 72, net of interest under section 84:

the total amount of interest that the Commissioner would be liable for under section 84 on contributions described in paragraphs (a) and (b), excluding amounts described in paragraph (a)﻿(i) to (iv) on a first-in first-out basis. For the purposes of calculating the amount of interest payable on the relevant contributions, the interest period in section 87 is treated as the number of days in the period—
beginning on the day the Commissioner received the contribution or the provider received the contribution (if the contribution was not via the Commissioner):
ending with the day that the Commissioner pays the refund amount under this section.

The refund amount must be paid to the person, their employer, the Crown, and any other person making a contribution in respect of the person, in proportion to the Commissioner’s best estimate of what they contributed, taking into account amounts described in subsection (3)﻿(a)﻿(i) to (iv).

When the Commissioner has paid the refund amount, the amount of member’s accumulation for the person previously paid to the Commissioner (the accumulation money), and the contributions held by the Commissioner in respect of the person in the holding account described in section 72 including interest under section 84 (the holding account money) are treated in the following ways:
the accumulation money and the holding account money are public money, and are not trust money for the purposes of sections 66 to 68 of the Public Finance Act 1989:
subpart 2 of Part 3 does not apply to the accumulation money and holding account money, and the Commissioner must pay the money into the Crown Bank account.

history.history-note: Section 59D: inserted (with effect on 1 July 2007), on 19 December 2007, by section 47 of the Taxation (KiwiSaver) Act 2007 (2007 No 110).

Section 59D(1)﻿(a): replaced, on 1 April 2020, by section 18(2) of the Taxation (KiwiSaver, Student Loans, and Remedial Matters) Act 2020 (2020 No 5).

Section 59D(2)﻿(b): replaced, on 1 July 2013, by section 184(2) of the Taxation (Annual Rates, Trans-Tasman Savings Portability, KiwiSaver, and Remedial Matters) Act 2010 (2010 No 109).

Section 59D(3)﻿(a)﻿(iv): inserted, on 1 July 2013, by section 184(3) of the Taxation (Annual Rates, Trans-Tasman Savings Portability, KiwiSaver, and Remedial Matters) Act 2010 (2010 No 109).
Section 59D(3)﻿(c): amended, on 1 July 2013, by section 184(4) of the Taxation (Annual Rates, Trans-Tasman Savings Portability, KiwiSaver, and Remedial Matters) Act 2010 (2010 No 109).
Section 59D(4): amended, on 1 July 2013, by section 184(5) of the Taxation (Annual Rates, Trans-Tasman Savings Portability, KiwiSaver, and Remedial Matters) Act 2010 (2010 No 109).

subpart.heading: Deductions of contributions from salary or wages
subpart.crosshead: Payments of salary or wages to which deduction rules apply

prov.heading: Application of subpart

para.text: This subpart applies to an employer in respect of any employee to whom 1 or more of the following applies:
the employee has started new employment with the employer and the automatic enrolment rules apply:
the employee has notified his or her employer of their KiwiSaver status and of any changes in that status:
the Commissioner has given the employer a notice requiring the deduction of contributions from the employee’s salary or wages.
This subpart applies to all payments of salary or wages—
after the employee starts that new employment (in a case to which subsection (1)﻿(a) applies); or
that are calculated by the employer after the employer receives that notice (in a case to which subsection (1)﻿(b) or (c) applies).

history.history-note: Section 60(1)﻿(b): amended, on 1 April 2019, by section 380 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2017–18, Employment and Investment Income, and Remedial Matters) Act 2018 (2018 No 5).

prov.heading: Commissioner may give notice

para.text: The Commissioner may give a notice to an employer requiring the deduction of contributions in order to achieve the effect of section 15 or section 36 (including if the employer fails to comply with section 23).

prov.heading: When subpart does not apply

para.text: This subpart does not apply to an employer in respect of an employee, or to a payment of salary or wages,—
if section 20(2) has required the employer to stop making deductions after an opt-out; or

if the employee has given or shown the employer a notice of a savings suspension, or the Commissioner has notified the employer of a savings suspension, that has been granted under subpart 4, for so long as the employer is satisfied that the employee is on that savings suspension; or
for the period that the employee has a valid non-deduction notice that they have given to their employer under section 112B; or

if, in accordance with the PAYE rules, no tax deduction is required to be made from the payment of salary or wages at the time the payment is made and the payment is not salary and wages for a private domestic worker.

history.history-note: Section 62(b): amended, on 1 April 2019, by section 321 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2018–19, Modernising Tax Administration, and Remedial Matters) Act 2019 (2019 No 5).
Section 62(bb): inserted (with effect on 1 July 2012), on 2 November 2012, by section 230 of the Taxation (Annual Rates, Returns Filing, and Remedial Matters) Act 2012 (2012 No 88).
Section 62(c): amended (with effect on 1 July 2007), on 19 December 2007, by section 48 of the Taxation (KiwiSaver) Act 2007 (2007 No 110).

prov.heading: Part also applies to PAYE intermediaries

para.text: This Part applies to a PAYE intermediary (within the meaning of section YA 1 of the Income Tax Act 2007) who is acting under sections RP 1 to RP 16 of that Act as if references to the employer were a reference to the PAYE intermediary and with other necessary modifications.

history.history-note: Section 63: amended, on 1 April 2008, by section ZA 2(1) of the Income Tax Act 2007 (2007 No 97).

prov.label: 63A
prov.heading: How subpart applies to private domestic workers

para.text: For the purposes of this subpart, a private domestic worker who is an employer under paragraph (b) of the definition of employer is treated as making payments of salary or wages to themselves in the capacity of employee. Consequently, the private domestic worker may be both employer and employee.

history.history-note: Section 63A: inserted (with effect on 1 July 2007), on 19 December 2007, by section 49 of the Taxation (KiwiSaver) Act 2007 (2007 No 110).

prov.label: 63B
prov.heading: Information on salary or wages from which deductions are made

para.text: An employer who makes a deduction under this subpart must specify the amount of salary or wages from which the deduction was made in their employment income information for the payment of salary or wages from which the deduction was made, if—
that amount differs from the information required under schedule 4, table 1, row 4, item d of the Tax Administration Act 1994; and
the deduction is the first that the employer has made under this subpart for the relevant employee.

history.history-note: Section 63B: inserted, on 1 April 2020, by section 19 of the Taxation (KiwiSaver, Student Loans, and Remedial Matters) Act 2020 (2020 No 5).

prov.heading: Contribution rate

para.text: The contribution rate, in relation to an employee and to an employer and to each payment of salary or wages, is—
3.5% of the employee's gross salary or wages, if—
section 60(1)﻿(a), (b) or (c) first applied in respect of the employee on or after 1 April 2009 and the employee has not given his or her employer a notice under subsection (2); or
section 66A applied in respect of the employee immediately before 1 April 2009; or
the employee is on a 2% contribution rate immediately before the first pay period that starts on or after 1 April 2013 because they chose 2% under subsection (2); or

4% of the employee's gross salary or wages, if section 60(1)﻿(a), (b) or (c) first applied in respect of the employee before 1 April 2009 and the employee has not given his or her employer a notice under paragraph (b); or
8% of the employee’s gross salary or wages if the employee gives his or her employer a notice requiring contributions to be deducted at that rate; or
3% of the employee’s gross salary or wages if the payment of salary or wages is calculated on or after 1 April 2026 and the employee has a rate reduction under subpart 3B.
Despite subsection (1), the employee may choose a contribution rate of 3.5%, 4%, 6%, 8%, or 10% of their gross salary or wages by giving notice of the rate they choose to—
their employer; or
their KiwiSaver scheme provider; or
the Commissioner.
If an employee gives a notice under subsection (2) to the Commissioner, the notice must include any other information that the Commissioner requires.
A provider must, as soon as practicable after receiving a notice under subsection (2) in relation to an employee, give notice to the Commissioner of—
the employee’s name and address; and
the employee’s tax file number; and
the contribution rate chosen by the employee; and
any other information that the Commissioner requires.
The Commissioner must, as soon as practicable after receiving a notice under subsection (2) or (2C) in relation to an employee, give notice to each of the person’s employers of—
the employee’s name; and
the employee’s tax file number; and
the contribution rate chosen by the employee.
The new rate applies to the next payment of salary or wages that is calculated after the employer receives the notice under subsection (2) or (2D).

For a contribution rate under subsection (1)﻿(a), when a change occurs to a rate in this Act, or in regulations made under this Act, affecting the contribution that must be deducted from a payment of salary or wages, the calculation of the amount of the contribution must be made at the rate applying on the day on which the salary or wages are paid.
An employee may not change his or her contribution rate in relation to an employer at intervals that are less than 92 days apart unless the employer agrees.

history.history-note: Section 64(1)﻿(a): substituted, on 1 April 2009, by section 43(1) of the Taxation (Urgent Measures and Annual Rates) Act 2008 (2008 No 105).
Section 64(1)﻿(a): amended, on 1 April 2026, by section 20(1) of the Taxation (Budget Measures) Act 2025 (2025 No 26).

Section 64(1)﻿(a)﻿(iii): inserted, on 1 April 2013 (applying for payments of salary or wages for pay periods that start on or after that date), by section 231(2) of the Taxation (Annual Rates, Returns Filing, and Remedial Matters) Act 2012 (2012 No 88).
Section 64(1)﻿(ab): inserted, on 1 April 2009, by section 43(1) of the Taxation (Urgent Measures and Annual Rates) Act 2008 (2008 No 105).
Section 64(1)﻿(c): inserted, on 1 February 2026, by section 20(3) of the Taxation (Budget Measures) Act 2025 (2025 No 26).
Section 64(2): replaced, on 1 April 2022, by section 20(1) of the Taxation (KiwiSaver, Student Loans, and Remedial Matters) Act 2020 (2020 No 5).
Section 64(2): amended, on 1 April 2026, by section 20(1) of the Taxation (Budget Measures) Act 2025 (2025 No 26).
Section 64(2B): inserted, on 1 April 2022, by section 20(1) of the Taxation (KiwiSaver, Student Loans, and Remedial Matters) Act 2020 (2020 No 5).
Section 64(2C): inserted, on 1 April 2022, by section 20(1) of the Taxation (KiwiSaver, Student Loans, and Remedial Matters) Act 2020 (2020 No 5).
Section 64(2D): inserted, on 1 April 2022, by section 20(1) of the Taxation (KiwiSaver, Student Loans, and Remedial Matters) Act 2020 (2020 No 5).
Section 64(3): replaced, on 1 April 2022, by section 20(1) of the Taxation (KiwiSaver, Student Loans, and Remedial Matters) Act 2020 (2020 No 5).
Section 64(3B): inserted, on 1 April 2018, by section 381 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2017–18, Employment and Investment Income, and Remedial Matters) Act 2018 (2018 No 5).
Section 64(4): amended, on 1 April 2020, by section 20(2) of the Taxation (KiwiSaver, Student Loans, and Remedial Matters) Act 2020 (2020 No 5).

prov.heading: Contribution rates may be changed by Order in Council

para.text: The Governor-General may, by Order in Council, do either or both of the following:
alter a rate specified in section 64:
provide for additional rates at which employees may contribute under this subpart, instead of at the rates under that section.
The Order in Council must state the date from which the rate or rates is to have effect (which must be the first day of a tax year (as defined in section YA 1 of the Income Tax Act 2007)).
The Order in Council must state how it will apply (for example, whether it applies to the persons to whom this subpart already applies).
An order under this section—
is secondary legislation (see Part 3 of the Legislation Act 2019 for publication requirements); and
must be confirmed by an Act (see subpart 3 of Part 5 of the Legislation Act 2019).
Every Order in Council made under this section has the force of law as if it were enacted by this Act.

legtable.summary: The following table is small in size and has 3 columns that are grouped under the heading Legislation Act 2019 requirements for secondary legislation made under this section.

entry.emphasis: Legislation Act 2019 requirements for secondary legislation made under this section

row.entry: PCO must publish it on the legislation website and notify it in the Gazette
The Minister must present it to the House of Representatives
It may be disallowed by the House of Representatives

entry.emphasis: This note is not part of the Act.

history.history-note: Section 65(1)﻿(a): amended, on 1 April 2009, by section 44 of the Taxation (Urgent Measures and Annual Rates) Act 2008 (2008 No 105).
Section 65(2): amended, on 1 April 2008, by section ZA 2(1) of the Income Tax Act 2007 (2007 No 97).
Section 65(4): inserted, on 28 October 2021, by section 3 of the Secondary Legislation Act 2021 (2021 No 7).
Section 65(4): repealed, on 1 January 2016, by section 14 of the Legislation (Confirmable Instruments) Amendment Act 2015 (2015 No 120).
Section 65(6): repealed, on 1 January 2016, by section 14 of the Legislation (Confirmable Instruments) Amendment Act 2015 (2015 No 120).

prov.label: 65A
prov.heading: Orders are confirmable instruments

history.history-note: Section 65A: repealed, on 28 October 2021, by section 3 of the Secondary Legislation Act 2021 (2021 No 7).

prov.heading: Obligation to make deductions: general rule

para.text: The employer must make deductions of contributions from each payment of the employee’s gross salary or wages of an amount equal to the contribution rate.

history.history-note: Section 66: substituted, on 1 April 2008, by section 50 of the Taxation (KiwiSaver) Act 2007 (2007 No 110).
Section 66: amended, on 1 April 2009, by section 45 of the Taxation (Urgent Measures and Annual Rates) Act 2008 (2008 No 105).

prov.label: 66A
prov.heading: Obligation to make deductions: transitional rule

history.history-note: Section 66A: repealed, on 1 April 2009, by section 46 of the Taxation (Urgent Measures and Annual Rates) Act 2008 (2008 No 105).

prov.heading: PAYE rules apply to deductions

para.text: The PAYE rules apply to the deduction of contributions under this subpart, as far as applicable and with the necessary modifications, as if—
every reference to income tax were a reference to contributions; and
every reference to amounts of tax withheld were a reference to the deduction of contributions; and
every reference to a tax code were a reference to a deduction rate; and
every reference to an amount required to be deducted under the PAYE rules were a reference to an amount required to be deducted under this Act.
Every employer and employee must comply with the requirements of the PAYE rules to the extent to which those rules apply under this section.
However, the following do not apply to any amount required to be deducted under this subpart:
sections BC 1, LA 6, RA 2, RD 9, RD 10, RD 10B, RD 13, RD 16, RD 17, and RD 18 of the Income Tax Act 2007 and subpart 3D and schedule 5 of the Tax Administration Act 1994; and
sections 139C, 140 to 140DB, 141FD, 141JA, 142E, 144, and 150B of the Tax Administration Act 1994.
Any deduction made under this subpart is not part of or included in any amount of tax withheld under the PAYE rules on account of income tax.
The deductions made under this subpart are in addition to any amounts of tax required to be withheld under the PAYE rules.
This section is subject to sections 212 to 216.

history.history-note: Section 67(1)﻿(b): amended, on 1 April 2008, by section ZA 2(1) of the Income Tax Act 2007 (2007 No 97).
Section 67(3)﻿(a): amended, on 1 April 2019, by section 382(2) of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2017–18, Employment and Investment Income, and Remedial Matters) Act 2018 (2018 No 5).
Section 67(3)﻿(a): amended, on 1 April 2018, by section 382(1) of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2017–18, Employment and Investment Income, and Remedial Matters) Act 2018 (2018 No 5).
Section 67(3)﻿(a): amended, on 1 April 2008, by section ZA 2(1) of the Income Tax Act 2007 (2007 No 97).
Section 67(4): amended, on 1 April 2008, by section ZA 2(1) of the Income Tax Act 2007 (2007 No 97).
Section 67(5): amended, on 1 April 2008, by section ZA 2(1) of the Income Tax Act 2007 (2007 No 97).

prov.heading: Money paid for things other than retirement benefits does not count as contribution under this Act

para.text: This section applies if money is paid to a provider of a KiwiSaver scheme in respect of all or any of—
the provision of retirement benefits for the member of the scheme; and
other things (for example, life insurance premiums).
The money paid in respect of the other things—
does not count as a contribution under this Act or towards the contribution rate; and
cannot be paid via the Commissioner.
This subpart does not require an employer to make deductions from salary or wages in respect of that money.
This section does not apply to permitted withdrawals.

prov.heading: Unremitted deductions made by employers

para.text: This section applies if—
an amount is included in employment income information received by the Commissioner as a deduction made by an employer under this subpart; and
the Commissioner is satisfied that the employer has deducted the amount under this subpart; and

the amount of the deduction is not paid to the Commissioner by the employer on or before the date by which the employer is required to pay the deduction to the Commissioner under section RD 4 of the Income Tax Act 2007 (as applied by section 67 of this Act).
The amount of the deduction is treated, for the purposes of this Act, as having been received by the Commissioner,—
for employment income information that includes the date of the payday on which the deduction was made, on that date; or
otherwise, on the 15th day of the month in which the deduction was made.

history.history-note: Section 69: replaced, on 1 April 2020, by section 21 of the Taxation (KiwiSaver, Student Loans, and Remedial Matters) Act 2020 (2020 No 5).

prov.heading: Unexplained remittances of deductions received from employers

para.text: This section applies if—
the Commissioner receives an amount (the received amount) from an employer in relation to the amounts deducted by the employer under this subpart; and
the employer has failed to supply to the Commissioner the particulars required by the Commissioner in relation to those amounts deducted; and

the Commissioner is unable to ascertain to the Commissioner’s satisfaction, in sufficient time prior to the cut-off day for the making of on-payments to the providers of KiwiSaver schemes, the portion of the received amount attributable to each of the persons from whom an amount was deducted by the employer.

The Commissioner may, for the purposes of this Part, hold the received amount until the amount attributable to each of the persons from whom an amount has been deducted by the employer has been established to the satisfaction of the Commissioner.

prov.heading: Time at which unexplained remittances of deductions deemed to be received

para.text: Any amount that is held by the Commissioner under section 70(2) is treated, for the purposes of this Act (other than sections 84 to 91 (interest on contributions) and clause 8 of the KiwiSaver scheme rules (withdrawal for purpose of purchase of first home)), as not having been received by the Commissioner until the day on which the amount attributable to each of the persons from whom an amount has been deducted by the employer has been established to the satisfaction of the Commissioner.

history.history-note: Section 71 heading: replaced, on 1 April 2020, by section 22 of the Taxation (KiwiSaver, Student Loans, and Remedial Matters) Act 2020 (2020 No 5).

prov.heading: Inland Revenue KiwiSaver Holding Account

para.text: The Commissioner must establish a memorandum account, called the Inland Revenue KiwiSaver Holding Account (the holding account), for the purpose of recording the receipt, deduction, payment, and refund of contributions and interest under this Act.
The holding account established under subsection (1) is not a facility for the purposes of the Financial Transactions Reporting Act 1996.

prov.heading: Certain unremitted deductions and employer contributions entered in and paid out of holding account

para.text: This section applies to any amount that—
is included in employment income information provided under section RD 22 of the Income Tax Act 2007 as—
a deduction made from an employee’s salary or wages under subpart 1; or
an employer contribution for an employee; and
is not received by the Commissioner at the same time as, or before, the Commissioner receives the information described in paragraph (a); and
the Commissioner is satisfied,—
for an amount included in the information as a deduction, has been deducted from salary or wages under subpart 1:

for an amount included in the information as an employer contribution, that the employer to whom the information relates has sufficient funds available, at the time at which that employer makes the payment of salary or wages to which the contribution relates, to be able to pay to the Commissioner.

For the purposes of subsection (1)﻿(a)﻿(ii), an amount of an employer contribution for an employee includes a contribution payable on or before 31 March 2020 that remains unpaid on 1 April 2020 in relation to which the relevant information referred to in subsection (1)﻿(b) and (c) has been provided to the Commissioner.

As soon as practicable after receiving employment income information in relation to an amount described in subsection (1), the Commissioner must enter the amount in the holding account in respect of the person, as applicable,—
from whose salary or wages the deduction was made; or
whom the employer contribution is for.

Subject to sections 75 and 77, as soon as practicable after entering an amount in the holding account under this section, the Commissioner must pay the amount to the provider of the relevant KiwiSaver scheme, without further authority than this section.
Money entered in the holding account under this section is not trust money for the purposes of sections 66 to 68 of the Public Finance Act 1989.
This section is subject to sections 70, 71, 95C, and 95D.

history.history-note: Section 73 heading: replaced, on 1 April 2020, by section 23(1) of the Taxation (KiwiSaver, Student Loans, and Remedial Matters) Act 2020 (2020 No 5).
Section 73(1): replaced, on 1 April 2020, by section 23(2) of the Taxation (KiwiSaver, Student Loans, and Remedial Matters) Act 2020 (2020 No 5).
Section 73(1B): inserted, on 30 March 2022, by section 251 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2021–22, GST, and Remedial Matters) Act 2022 (2022 No 10).
Section 73(2): replaced, on 1 April 2020, by section 23(2) of the Taxation (KiwiSaver, Student Loans, and Remedial Matters) Act 2020 (2020 No 5).
Section 73(3): amended, on 1 April 2020, by section 23(3) of the Taxation (KiwiSaver, Student Loans, and Remedial Matters) Act 2020 (2020 No 5).
Section 73(3): amended (with effect on 1 July 2007), on 19 December 2007, by section 51 of the Taxation (KiwiSaver) Act 2007 (2007 No 110).
Section 73(5): amended, on 1 April 2020, by section 23(4) of the Taxation (KiwiSaver, Student Loans, and Remedial Matters) Act 2020 (2020 No 5).
Section 73(6): repealed, on 1 April 2020, by section 23(5) of the Taxation (KiwiSaver, Student Loans, and Remedial Matters) Act 2020 (2020 No 5).

prov.heading: Other contributions entered in and paid out of holding account

para.text: This section applies to any amount of contribution that is received by the Commissioner other than an amount referred to in section 73.
As soon as practicable after receiving the amount, the Commissioner must enter that amount in the holding account in respect of the person to whom the contribution relates.

Subject to sections 75 and 77, as soon as practicable after entering an amount in the holding account under this section, the Commissioner must pay the amount to the provider of the relevant KiwiSaver scheme.
Money entered in the holding account under this section is trust money for the purposes of sections 66 to 68 of the Public Finance Act 1989.

history.history-note: Section 74(3): amended, on 1 April 2020, by section 24 of the Taxation (KiwiSaver, Student Loans, and Remedial Matters) Act 2020 (2020 No 5).

prov.heading: Initial contributions stay in holding account for 62 days

para.text: This section applies to all contributions received by the Commissioner in respect of a person in the 62-day period starting on the earlier of—
the day on which the Commissioner receives the first contribution in respect of the person:
the day on which the Commissioner is given notice or otherwise knows that the person is a member of a KiwiSaver scheme.
The Commissioner must hold those contributions in the holding account.

The Commissioner must pay those contributions to the provider of the person’s KiwiSaver scheme as soon as practicable after the expiry of that 62-day period (without further authority than this section where those contributions meet the requirements of section 73(1)).
The Commissioner must give notice to the person as soon as practicable after paying those contributions to the provider of the person’s KiwiSaver scheme.
The provider must give notice to the person as soon as practicable after receiving those contributions.
Subsection (3) is subject to section 77.

history.history-note: Section 75 heading: amended, on 1 April 2020, by section 25(1) of the Taxation (KiwiSaver, Student Loans, and Remedial Matters) Act 2020 (2020 No 5).
Section 75(1): substituted (with effect on 1 July 2007), on 19 December 2007, by section 52(1) of the Taxation (KiwiSaver) Act 2007 (2007 No 110).
Section 75(1): amended, on 1 April 2020, by section 25(2) of the Taxation (KiwiSaver, Student Loans, and Remedial Matters) Act 2020 (2020 No 5).
Section 75(3): amended, on 1 April 2020, by section 25(3) of the Taxation (KiwiSaver, Student Loans, and Remedial Matters) Act 2020 (2020 No 5).
Section 75(3): amended (with effect on 1 July 2007), on 19 December 2007, by section 52(2) of the Taxation (KiwiSaver) Act 2007 (2007 No 110).

prov.heading: Employer contributions may stay in holding account until deducted contributions paid

history.history-note: Section 76: repealed, on 1 April 2020, by section 26 of the Taxation (KiwiSaver, Student Loans, and Remedial Matters) Act 2020 (2020 No 5).

prov.heading: Small amounts of contributions may be held until big enough to be on-paid

para.text: This section applies if the Commissioner and the provider of a KiwiSaver scheme agree on a minimum threshold for the payment of contributions to the provider.
The Commissioner may hold in the holding account any amount of contribution that relates to a person until the amount meets that minimum threshold.
Then the Commissioner must pay that amount to the provider of the person’s KiwiSaver scheme (without further authority than this section where that amount meets the requirements of section 73(1)).

history.history-note: Section 77(3): amended (with effect on 1 July 2007), on 19 December 2007, by section 53 of the Taxation (KiwiSaver) Act 2007 (2007 No 110).

prov.heading: Treatment of certain unremitted deductions and employer contributions in holding account

para.text: This section applies to an amount referred to in section 73(1) to the extent to which it is not paid to the Commissioner on or before the date by which the employer is required to pay the deduction or employer contribution to the Commissioner under, as applicable,—
section RD 4 of the Income Tax Act 2007 (as applied by section 67 of this Act); or
section 93(3) or (4), as applicable.
The Commissioner must pay the amount out of a Crown Bank Account, without further authority than this section.
The amount is treated, for the purposes of section 73, as having been received by the Commissioner,—

for employment income information received by the Commissioner that includes the date of the payday for the payment of salary or wages to which the deduction or employer contribution relates, on that date; or
for an employer contribution referred to in section 73(1B), on 1 April 2020; or
otherwise, on the 15th day of the month in which the payment of salary or wages to which the deduction or employer contribution relates was made.

history.history-note: Section 78: replaced, on 1 April 2020, by section 27 of the Taxation (KiwiSaver, Student Loans, and Remedial Matters) Act 2020 (2020 No 5).
Section 78(3)﻿(ab): inserted, on 30 March 2022, by section 252 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2021–22, GST, and Remedial Matters) Act 2022 (2022 No 10).

prov.heading: Information that Commissioner must supply to providers when paying contributions

para.text: The Commissioner must supply, to the provider of a KiwiSaver scheme to which the Commissioner makes any payment under this subpart, any information, and in any format, that the Commissioner determines after consultation with the provider of the KiwiSaver scheme.

prov.heading: Refund by Commissioner of amounts paid in excess of required amount of deduction or if employee opts out

para.text: The Commissioner may refund any amount of contribution to a person in relation to whom a contribution was made, or from whose salary or wages the amount was deducted if—
the person opts out and the contribution is in the possession of the Commissioner; or
the contribution is in excess of the amount that this Act requires to be deducted and the contribution is in the possession of the Commissioner; or
the person has opted out and the contribution was deducted from salary or wages but was not refunded to the person or paid to the Commissioner.
However, if a request is made under section 173L of the Tax Administration Act 1994, the Commissioner may apply any amount of that contribution in accordance with that request.
This section is subject to section 91B.

history.history-note: Section 80(1): amended (with effect on 1 July 2007), on 19 December 2007, by section 54 of the Taxation (KiwiSaver) Act 2007 (2007 No 110).
Section 80(3): inserted, on 30 March 2022, by section 253 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2021–22, GST, and Remedial Matters) Act 2022 (2022 No 10).

prov.heading: Refund by provider of amounts paid in excess of required amount of contribution or if member opts out

para.text: A provider must refund to the Commissioner any amount of contribution paid to the provider by the Commissioner in respect of a member of that provider’s KiwiSaver scheme if—
the member opts out; or
the contribution is in excess of the amount that is required to be paid to the provider under the KiwiSaver scheme and this Act.
The Commissioner must refund or give credit for any amount refunded under subsection (1) in the manner that the Commissioner thinks fit.
However, if a request is made under section 173L of the Tax Administration Act 1994, the Commissioner may apply any amount of that contribution in accordance with that request.
This section is subject to section 91B.

history.history-note: Section 81 heading: replaced, on 1 April 2020, by section 28(1) of the Taxation (KiwiSaver, Student Loans, and Remedial Matters) Act 2020 (2020 No 5).
Section 81(1): replaced, on 1 April 2020, by section 28(2) of the Taxation (KiwiSaver, Student Loans, and Remedial Matters) Act 2020 (2020 No 5).
Section 81(2): amended, on 1 April 2020, by section 28(3) of the Taxation (KiwiSaver, Student Loans, and Remedial Matters) Act 2020 (2020 No 5).
Section 81(4): inserted, on 30 March 2022, by section 254 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2021–22, GST, and Remedial Matters) Act 2022 (2022 No 10).

prov.label: 81B
prov.heading: Residual refunds

para.text: If the Commissioner can not process an amount held in the holding account in accordance with this Act, or the amount is in excess of what this Act or a Revenue Act requires to be in the holding account, then the Commissioner may refund the amount to the person that the Commissioner considers has the best claim to it.
This section is subject to section 91B.

history.history-note: Section 81B: inserted, on 29 August 2011, by section 211 of the Taxation (Tax Administration and Remedial Matters) Act 2011 (2011 No 63).
Section 81B(2): inserted, on 30 March 2022, by section 255 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2021–22, GST, and Remedial Matters) Act 2022 (2022 No 10).

prov.heading: Trustee investment rules do not apply to contributions in holding account

para.text: Sections 30, 58, 59, 128, and 129 of the Trusts Act 2019 do not apply to the Commissioner in respect of money in the holding account.

history.history-note: Section 82: amended, on 30 January 2021, by section 161 of the Trusts Act 2019 (2019 No 38).

prov.heading: Unclaimed money held by Commissioner

para.text: This section applies to any money—
that has been in the Commissioner’s possession under this Act for a period of no less than 5 years; and
about which the Commissioner has insufficient information in order to process that money in accordance with this Act.
No interest is payable on that money under this subpart in respect of the period that this section applies.
The Unclaimed Money Act 1971, and not sections 70 and 74 of the Public Finance Act 1989, applies to that money—
as if the Commissioner is the holder of the money for the purposes of the Unclaimed Money Act 1971; and

for money relating to an employer deduction or employer contribution to which subsection (1)﻿(b) applies, as if the date on which the Commissioner becomes the holder is the last day of the month to which the employment income information for the money relates; and
as if the money must be processed in accordance with this Act, and not paid to the owner, if a valid claim is made to the money; and
as if the words “more than $100” in the words before the subparagraphs in section 4(3)﻿(a) of the Unclaimed Money Act 1971 were replaced with the words “1 cent or more”; and
with other necessary modifications.

If the Commissioner enters into a special arrangement under section 9 of that Act, the Minister who, under the authority of any warrant or with the authority of the Prime Minister, is responsible for the administration of the Unclaimed Money Act 1971 must carry out, after consultation with the Minister of Finance, the responsibilities that would otherwise have been carried out by the Commissioner under that Act.

history.history-note: Section 83(1)﻿(a): amended, on 30 March 2021, by section 193(1) of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2020–21, Feasibility Expenditure, and Remedial Matters) Act 2021 (2021 No 8).
Section 83(3)﻿(ab): inserted, on 30 March 2021, by section 193(2) of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2020–21, Feasibility Expenditure, and Remedial Matters) Act 2021 (2021 No 8).
Section 83(3)﻿(c): replaced (with effect on 30 March 2021), on 30 March 2022, by section 256 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2021–22, GST, and Remedial Matters) Act 2022 (2022 No 10).

prov.heading: Interest on money in holding account

para.text: The Commissioner is liable to pay interest in accordance with sections 85 to 91 on any amount of contribution that is received, or treated as received, by the Commissioner in respect of a person under this Act.
Sections 68(2) and 69 of the Public Finance Act 1989 do not apply to the holding account.

Despite subsection (1), the Commissioner is not liable to pay interest on any amount described in that subsection, if the relevant person has notified the Commissioner in writing of their wish to not be paid interest.

history.history-note: Section 84(2): amended, on 19 December 2007, by section 56(1) of the Taxation (KiwiSaver) Act 2007 (2007 No 110).
Section 84(3): added, on 1 April 2009, by section 56(2) of the Taxation (KiwiSaver) Act 2007 (2007 No 110).

prov.heading: Time when deductions and employer contributions treated as received for interest purposes

para.text: An amount referred to in section 73(1) is treated, for the purpose of the payment of interest, as received by the Commissioner, as applicable,—
for employment income information that includes the date of the payday on which the deduction was made, on that date if the Commissioner is satisfied that the deduction has been made on that date; or
for employment income information that includes the date of the payday for the payment of salary or wages to which the employer contribution relates, on that date; or
for an employer contribution referred to in section 73(1B), on 1 April 2020; or
otherwise, on the 15th day of the month in which the payment of salary or wages to which the deduction or employer contribution relates was made.

history.history-note: Section 85: replaced, on 1 April 2020, by section 29 of the Taxation (KiwiSaver, Student Loans, and Remedial Matters) Act 2020 (2020 No 5).
