Section 85(1): repealed, on 30 March 2022, by section 257(1) of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2021–22, GST, and Remedial Matters) Act 2022 (2022 No 10).
Section 85(2): amended, on 30 March 2022, by section 257(2)﻿(a) of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2021–22, GST, and Remedial Matters) Act 2022 (2022 No 10).
Section 85(2)﻿(bb): inserted, on 30 March 2022, by section 257(2)﻿(b) of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2021–22, GST, and Remedial Matters) Act 2022 (2022 No 10).

prov.heading: Interest rate

para.text: The interest rate at which interest is payable under sections 84 to 91 is—

eqn.eqn-line: Commissioner’s paying rate × (1 − lowest tax rate)

para.text: where—

Commissioner’s paying rate is the rate of interest established and notified as the Commissioner’s paying rate by an Order in Council made under section 120H of the Tax Administration Act 1994 as the Commissioner’s paying rate applying on the day on which the contribution is received or treated as received
lowest tax rate is the tax rate in schedule 1, part D, table 2, row 7, column 3 of the Income Tax Act 2007.

The interest rate calculated using the formula is expressed as a percentage, rounded to 2 decimal places, with numbers at the midpoint or greater being rounded up and other numbers being rounded down.

history.history-note: Section 86(1) lowest tax rate: substituted, on 1 April 2008, by section ZA 2(1) of the Income Tax Act 2007 (2007 No 97).

Section 86(1) lowest tax rate: amended (with effect on 1 April 2010), on 7 September 2010 (applying for the 2010–11 and later tax years), by section 186(1) of the Taxation (Annual Rates, Trans-Tasman Savings Portability, KiwiSaver, and Remedial Matters) Act 2010 (2010 No 109).
Section 86(2): added, on 19 December 2007, by section 58 of the Taxation (KiwiSaver) Act 2007 (2007 No 110).

prov.heading: Amount of interest payable

para.text: The amount of interest payable by the Commissioner in respect of a person is calculated in accordance with the following formula:
where—
contribution is the amount of contribution in respect of the person to whom the interest is payable

interest period is the number of days in the period that begins on the day on which the Commissioner receives, or is treated as receiving, the amount of contribution and ends with the day on which the Commissioner on-pays the amount of contribution to the provider of the person’s KiwiSaver scheme or refunds the amount under this Part (except section 81)
interest rate is the rate calculated under section 86.

prov.heading: How and when interest is paid on on-payments

para.text: Interest that is payable under section 84 on an amount of contribution that is on-paid to a provider must be paid to the provider of the person’s KiwiSaver scheme, in respect of the person, within 92 days of when the amount of contribution is on-paid to the provider.

history.history-note: Section 88: amended, on 1 April 2020, by section 30 of the Taxation (KiwiSaver, Student Loans, and Remedial Matters) Act 2020 (2020 No 5).
Section 88: amended, on 2 November 2012, by section 233 of the Taxation (Annual Rates, Returns Filing, and Remedial Matters) Act 2012 (2012 No 88).

prov.heading: How and when interest is paid on refunds

para.text: This section applies to interest that would be payable under section 84 in respect of an amount of contribution that is refunded under this Part (except section 81).
Interest must be paid with the refund.

history.history-note: Section 89(3): repealed, on 6 October 2009, by section 723 of the Taxation (International Taxation, Life Insurance, and Remedial Matters) Act 2009 (2009 No 34).
Section 89(4): repealed, on 6 October 2009, by section 723 of the Taxation (International Taxation, Life Insurance, and Remedial Matters) Act 2009 (2009 No 34).

prov.heading: Position if Commissioner’s paying rate changes

para.text: This section applies if the rate of interest established and notified as the Commissioner’s paying rate by an Order in Council made under section 120H of the Tax Administration Act 1994 changes during an interest period in respect of which interest is payable under section 84.
The Commissioner’s paying rate, for the purposes of section 86, must be taken to be the weighted-average rate based on the number of days on which each rate applied during that period.

prov.heading: Overpaid interest

para.text: Interest overpaid by the Commissioner under sections 84 to 90 may be recovered in the same manner as income tax that is payable under the Income Tax Act 2007.

history.history-note: Section 91: amended, on 1 April 2008, by section ZA 2(1) of the Income Tax Act 2007 (2007 No 97).
Heading: inserted, on 30 March 2022, by section 258 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2021–22, GST, and Remedial Matters) Act 2022 (2022 No 10).

prov.label: 91B
prov.heading: Time bar for refunding contributions

para.text: A provider must not refund an amount of contribution paid to the provider by the Commissioner in respect of a member of that provider’s KiwiSaver scheme that is in excess of the amount that is required to be paid to the provider under the KiwiSaver scheme and this Act if—
the amount is—
an amount that was deducted from the member’s salary or wages; or
an amount of employer contribution; and
the amount has arisen on an amended assessment; and
the 4-year period under section 108AB of the Tax Administration Act 1994 for amendment of an assessment has ended; and
the Commissioner has not requested the provider to refund the amount to the Commissioner.
The Commissioner must not refund an amount of contribution that is in excess of the amount that this Act requires to be deducted or paid if—
the amount is—
an amount that was deducted from a member’s salary or wages; or
an amount of employer contribution; and
the amount has arisen on an amended assessment; and
the 4-year period under section 108AB of the Tax Administration Act 1994 for amendment of an assessment has ended.

For the purposes of this section, the provision by a person of employment income information for an amount of a contribution for a payday is treated as the making of an assessment of the amount of the contribution by the person.

history.history-note: Section 91B: inserted, on 30 March 2022, by section 258 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2021–22, GST, and Remedial Matters) Act 2022 (2022 No 10).

subpart.heading: Contributions other than deductions from salary or wages

prov.heading: Application of this subpart

para.text: This subpart applies to contributions to a KiwiSaver scheme other than contributions deducted from salary or wages under subpart 1.

prov.label: 92A
prov.heading: How subpart applies to private domestic workers

para.text: For the purposes of this subpart, a private domestic worker who is an employer under paragraph (b) of the definition of employer is treated as making payments of salary or wages to themselves in the capacity of employee. Consequently, the private domestic worker may be both employer and employee.

history.history-note: Section 92A: inserted (with effect on 1 July 2007), on 19 December 2007, by section 59 of the Taxation (KiwiSaver) Act 2007 (2007 No 110).

prov.heading: Employer contributions paid via Commissioner

para.text: An employer must pay all amounts of employer contributions to the Commissioner.
The payment of an amount of employer contribution must be accompanied by the relevant employment income information.

If the employer is not a private domestic worker, the contribution must be paid to the Commissioner within the time prescribed in section RA 15 of the Income Tax Act 2007 for the payment of amounts of tax withheld relating to the payment of salary or wages to which the contribution relates, as if the contribution were an amount of tax.

If the employer is a private domestic worker, the contribution must be paid to the Commissioner within the time prescribed in sections RA 8, RA 10, and RD 4(4) of that Act for the payment of tax relating to the payment of salary or wages to which the contribution relates, as if the contribution were tax.
The employer must include details of employer contributions in respect of each employee in their employment income information for the payments of salary or wages to which the contribution relates.

For the purposes of the Tax Administration Act 1994, to the extent to which an employer fails to comply with subsection (5) in respect of an amount of employer contribution that the employer must pay to the Commissioner, that amount is treated as a short payment for the PAYE period for which the failure occurs.

history.history-note: Section 93: substituted, on 1 April 2008, by section 60 of the Taxation (KiwiSaver) Act 2007 (2007 No 110).
Section 93(2): amended, on 1 April 2019, by section 384(1) of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2017–18, Employment and Investment Income, and Remedial Matters) Act 2018 (2018 No 5).
Section 93(4): amended, on 1 April 2019, by section 384(2) of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2017–18, Employment and Investment Income, and Remedial Matters) Act 2018 (2018 No 5).
Section 93(5): amended, on 1 April 2020, by section 31 of the Taxation (KiwiSaver, Student Loans, and Remedial Matters) Act 2020 (2020 No 5).
Section 93(5): amended, on 1 April 2019, by section 384(3) of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2017–18, Employment and Investment Income, and Remedial Matters) Act 2018 (2018 No 5).

prov.heading: Employer must give notice that employer contributions to be paid via Commissioner

history.history-note: Section 94: repealed, on 1 July 2007, by section 65 of the Taxation (KiwiSaver and Company Tax Rate Amendments) Act 2007 (2007 No 19).

prov.heading: Contributions from persons other than employers may be paid via Commissioner

para.text: A person other than an employer (including a member of a KiwiSaver scheme) may make a contribution to a person’s (A’s) KiwiSaver scheme by paying it to the Commissioner provided that the contribution is accompanied by—
A’s name and address; and
A’s tax file number; and
any other information that the Commissioner may require.

prov.label: 95B
prov.heading: Unremitted employer contributions

para.text: This section applies if—
an amount is included in employment income information received by the Commissioner as an employer contribution for an employee; and

the Commissioner is satisfied that the employer to whom the information relates has sufficient funds available, at the time at which that employer makes the payment of salary or wages to which the contribution relates, to be able to pay the amount of the employer contribution to the Commissioner; and

the amount of the employer contribution is not paid to the Commissioner by that employer on or before the date by which that employer is required to pay the amount of employer contribution to the Commissioner under section 93(3) or (4), as applicable.
The amount of the employer contribution is treated, for the purposes of this Act, as having been received by the Commissioner,—
for employment income information that includes the date of the payday for the payment of salary or wages to which the contribution relates, on that date; or
otherwise, on the 15th day of the month in which the payment of salary or wages to which the contribution relates was made.

history.history-note: Section 95B: inserted, on 1 April 2020, by section 32 of the Taxation (KiwiSaver, Student Loans, and Remedial Matters) Act 2020 (2020 No 5).

prov.label: 95C
prov.heading: Unexplained remittances of employer contributions

para.text: This section applies if—
the Commissioner receives an amount (the received amount) of employer contributions from an employer; and
the employer has failed to supply to the Commissioner the particulars required by the Commissioner in relation to the received amount; and

the Commissioner is unable to ascertain to the Commissioner’s satisfaction, in sufficient time prior to the cut-off day for the making of on-payments to the providers of KiwiSaver schemes, the portion of the received amount attributable to each of the persons in relation to whom an employer contribution has been made by the employer.

The Commissioner may, for the purposes of this Part, hold the received amount until the amount attributable to each of the persons in relation to whom an employer contribution has been made by the employer has been established to the satisfaction of the Commissioner.

history.history-note: Section 95C: inserted, on 1 April 2020, by section 32 of the Taxation (KiwiSaver, Student Loans, and Remedial Matters) Act 2020 (2020 No 5).

prov.label: 95D
prov.heading: Time at which unexplained remittances of employer contributions deemed to be received

para.text: Any amount that is held by the Commissioner under section 95C(2) is treated, for the purposes of this Act (other than sections 84 to 91 (interest on contributions)), as not having been received by the Commissioner until the day on which the amount attributable to each of the persons in relation to whom an employer contribution has been made by the employer has been established to the satisfaction of the Commissioner.

history.history-note: Section 95D: inserted, on 1 April 2020, by section 32 of the Taxation (KiwiSaver, Student Loans, and Remedial Matters) Act 2020 (2020 No 5).

prov.heading: What Commissioner must do with contributions received, or treated as received, under this subpart

para.text: The Commissioner must, in accordance with subpart 2,—
first, pay the contribution into the holding account; and
secondly, on-pay the contribution to the provider of the person’s KiwiSaver scheme.
The payments required under subsection (1) must be made net of ESCT payable under the ESCT rules (if any).

history.history-note: Section 96 heading: replaced, on 1 April 2020, by section 33 of the Taxation (KiwiSaver, Student Loans, and Remedial Matters) Act 2020 (2020 No 5).
Section 96(2): amended, on 1 April 2008, by section ZA 2(1) of the Income Tax Act 2007 (2007 No 97).

prov.heading: Commissioner must give notice if employer contributions not remitted

para.text: This section applies for a PAYE period when—

an employer is required to provide a return in relation to an amount of employer contribution under this subpart, and includes a reference to the payment in their employment income information under the Tax Administration Act 1994; and
the Commissioner does not receive the payment in full by the due date for the payment.
The Commissioner must notify the employer that the payment has not been received.

history.history-note: Section 97: replaced, on 1 April 2019, by section 385 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2017–18, Employment and Investment Income, and Remedial Matters) Act 2018 (2018 No 5).

prov.heading: Short payments by employers if not enough money remitted to Commissioner to cover all of employees’ deductions and employer contributions

history.history-note: Section 98: repealed, on 1 April 2020, by section 34 of the Taxation (KiwiSaver, Student Loans, and Remedial Matters) Act 2020 (2020 No 5).

prov.label: 98A
prov.heading: Short payments of employer contributions

para.text: For the purposes of the Tax Administration Act 1994, an employer is treated as having an amount of short payment for a PAYE period equal to the difference between—
the amount of employer contribution that is received by the Commissioner for the PAYE period; and
the amount, for the PAYE period, of employer contribution included in their employment income information in accordance with this subpart.

history.history-note: Section 98A heading: replaced, on 1 April 2020, by section 35(1) of the Taxation (KiwiSaver, Student Loans, and Remedial Matters) Act 2020 (2020 No 5).
Section 98A: inserted, on 1 April 2008, by section 62 of the Taxation (KiwiSaver) Act 2007 (2007 No 110).
Section 98A: amended, on 1 April 2020, by section 35(2) of the Taxation (KiwiSaver, Student Loans, and Remedial Matters) Act 2020 (2020 No 5).
Section 98A(a): amended, on 1 April 2020, by section 35(3) of the Taxation (KiwiSaver, Student Loans, and Remedial Matters) Act 2020 (2020 No 5).
Section 98A(b): amended, on 1 April 2019, by section 387 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2017–18, Employment and Investment Income, and Remedial Matters) Act 2018 (2018 No 5).

prov.heading: Short payments if not enough employer contribution remitted to cover all employees

history.history-note: Section 99: repealed, on 1 April 2020, by section 36 of the Taxation (KiwiSaver, Student Loans, and Remedial Matters) Act 2020 (2020 No 5).

prov.heading: Refunds of employer contribution by Commissioner if employee opts out

para.text: If an employee opts out after an employer contribution is paid to the Commissioner, the Commissioner must, if it is still in his or her possession, refund the employer contribution to the employer.
This section is subject to section 91B.

history.history-note: Section 100(1): amended (with effect on 1 April 2008), on 6 October 2009, by section 724 of the Taxation (International Taxation, Life Insurance, and Remedial Matters) Act 2009 (2009 No 34).
Section 100(2): inserted, on 30 March 2022, by section 259 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2021–22, GST, and Remedial Matters) Act 2022 (2022 No 10).

prov.heading: Refunds of employer contribution by provider

para.text: The provider of a KiwiSaver scheme must refund to the Commissioner any amount of employer contribution that was paid under this Act by the Commissioner in excess of the amount of the employer contribution that this Act requires.
If an employee opts out after an employer contribution is paid to a provider by the Commissioner, the provider must refund the employer contribution to the Commissioner.
This section is subject to section 91B.

history.history-note: Section 101(1): amended, on 19 December 2007, by section 64(1)﻿(a) of the Taxation (KiwiSaver) Act 2007 (2007 No 110).
Section 101(1): amended, on 19 December 2007, by section 64(1)﻿(b) of the Taxation (KiwiSaver) Act 2007 (2007 No 110).
Section 101(1B): inserted, on 1 April 2020, by section 37 of the Taxation (KiwiSaver, Student Loans, and Remedial Matters) Act 2020 (2020 No 5).
Section 101(1C): inserted, on 30 March 2022, by section 260 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2021–22, GST, and Remedial Matters) Act 2022 (2022 No 10).
Section 101(2): repealed, on 19 December 2007, by section 64(2) of the Taxation (KiwiSaver) Act 2007 (2007 No 110).

prov.label: 101AA
prov.heading: What Commissioner must do with employer contribution refunded by provider

para.text: This section applies if an amount of employer contribution is refunded to the Commissioner by a provider under section 101(1B).
The Commissioner must—
refund the amount to a Crown Bank Account, if—
the amount has been paid out of a Crown Bank Account under section 78; and
the amount has not been paid to the Commissioner by the employer to whom the employer contribution relates; or
otherwise, refund the amount to the employer who made the employer contribution.
This section is subject to section 91B.

history.history-note: Section 101AA: inserted, on 1 April 2020, by section 38 of the Taxation (KiwiSaver, Student Loans, and Remedial Matters) Act 2020 (2020 No 5).
Section 101AA(3): inserted, on 30 March 2022, by section 261 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2021–22, GST, and Remedial Matters) Act 2022 (2022 No 10).

subpart.label: 3A
subpart.heading: Compulsory employer contributions to KiwiSaver schemes and complying superannuation funds

history.history-note: Subpart 3A: inserted, on 1 April 2008, by section 65 of the Taxation (KiwiSaver) Act 2007 (2007 No 110).

prov.label: 101A
prov.heading: General

para.text: An employer must pay, in accordance with sections 101E and 101F, a compulsory employer contribution for an employee, to the extent to which the employee meets the requirements in section 101C for a period to which a payment of salary or wages relates.
Section 101B provides rules relevant to parties to an employment relationship, and how they bargain in respect of compulsory employer contributions and associated matters.
Despite subsection (1), an employer does not have to pay a compulsory employer contribution as provided in sections 101FB and 101FC.
Section 101G provides rules relevant to providers who receive compulsory employer contributions directly or from the Commissioner.

The rest of this subpart provides rules relating to compulsory employer contributions to complying superannuation funds. Also, subpart 3 provides some rules for employer contributions to KiwiSaver schemes.

For the purposes of this subpart, a private domestic worker who is an employer under paragraph (c) of the definition of employer is treated as making payments of salary or wages to themselves in the capacity of employee. Consequently, for the purposes of this subpart, the private domestic worker may be both employer and employee, if the worker chooses.

history.history-note: Section 101A: inserted, on 1 April 2008, by section 65 of the Taxation (KiwiSaver) Act 2007 (2007 No 110).
Section 101A(1): amended, on 31 March 2026, by section 219 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2025–26, Compliance Simplification, and Remedial Measures) Act 2026 (2026 No 8).
Section 101A(2B): inserted (with effect on 1 April 2008), on 6 October 2009, by section 725 of the Taxation (International Taxation, Life Insurance, and Remedial Matters) Act 2009 (2009 No 34).

prov.label: 101B
prov.heading: Compulsory contributions must be paid on top of gross salary or wages except to extent that parties otherwise agree after 13 December 2007

para.text: The purpose of this section is to ensure that, for contractual arrangements of parties to an employment relationship (as defined in section 4(2) of the Employment Relations Act 2000), compulsory contributions are paid in addition to an employee’s gross salary or wages described in section 101D(3).
The contractual arrangements of parties to an employment relationship must not have the effect of defeating the purpose of this section described in subsection (1).
A contractual term or condition has no effect to the extent to which it is contrary to the purpose of this section described in subsection (1).

However, on and after 13 December 2007, parties to an employment relationship are free to agree contractual terms and conditions that disregard the purpose of this section described in subsection (1), and, to the extent of such agreement, subsections (1) to (3) do not apply, unless, in respect of the employer and employee,—
section 60(1)﻿(a), (b) or (c) first applies on or after the day of assent for the Taxation (Urgent Measures and Annual Rates) Act 2008; and
the contractual terms and conditions do not account for the amount of compulsory contributions the employer is required to pay.
In the circumstances described in subsection (4)﻿(a) and (b), despite subsection (4),—
compulsory contributions must be paid in addition to an employee’s gross salary or wages described in section 101D(3), in accordance with the purpose of this section described in subsection (1); and
subsections (2) and (3) apply.
For the avoidance of doubt,—

the duty of good faith described in section 4 of the Employment Relations Act 2000 always applies when parties to an employment relationship bargain for terms and conditions relating to compulsory contributions and associated matters.

In this section, compulsory contributions means an amount of employer contributions equal to the amount of compulsory employer contributions that would be required by this subpart in the absence of section 101D(5)﻿(a).

history.history-note: Section 101B: inserted, on 1 April 2008, by section 65 of the Taxation (KiwiSaver) Act 2007 (2007 No 110).
Section 101B(4): substituted, on 15 December 2008, by section 47 of the Taxation (Urgent Measures and Annual Rates) Act 2008 (2008 No 105).
Section 101B(4A): inserted, on 15 December 2008, by section 47 of the Taxation (Urgent Measures and Annual Rates) Act 2008 (2008 No 105).
Section 101B(5): substituted, on 10 September 2008, by section 10 of the Employment Relations (Breaks, Infant Feeding, and Other Matters) Amendment Act 2008 (2008 No 58).
Section 101B(5)﻿(b): repealed, on 16 December 2008, by section 10 of the Employment Relations Amendment Act 2008 (2008 No 106).

prov.label: 101C
prov.heading: Employee’s requirements

para.text: For the purposes of section 101A(1), the requirements are that the employee—
is paid salary or wages from which the employer deducts or is required to deduct an amount for the employee’s KiwiSaver scheme or complying superannuation fund; and
is aged 16 or over; and
is not entitled to withdraw an amount from a fund or scheme under clause 4 of the KiwiSaver scheme rules (which relates to lock-in of funds) or a rule the same as that clause; and
must not have made a withdrawal under clause 12B of the KiwiSaver scheme rules (which relates to life-shortening congenital condition withdrawals); and
is not a defined benefit scheme member.

history.history-note: Section 101C: inserted, on 1 April 2008, by section 65 of the Taxation (KiwiSaver) Act 2007 (2007 No 110).
Section 101C(b): amended, on 1 April 2026, by section 21 of the Taxation (Budget Measures) Act 2025 (2025 No 26).
Section 101C(c): amended, on 1 July 2019, by section 315 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2018–19, Modernising Tax Administration, and Remedial Matters) Act 2019 (2019 No 5).
Section 101C(cb): inserted, on 1 April 2020, by section 39 of the Taxation (KiwiSaver, Student Loans, and Remedial Matters) Act 2020 (2020 No 5).

prov.label: 101D
prov.heading: Compulsory employer contribution amount: general rule

para.text: The amount of a compulsory employer contribution is a positive amount calculated using the following formula:

eqn.eqn-line: (payment of gross salary or wages × CEC rate) − other contributions − hybrid schemes amount.

para.text: The items in the formula are defined in subsections (3) to (6).

Payment of gross salary or wages is the amount of a payment of gross salary or wages from which the employer deducts or is required to deduct an amount for the employee’s KiwiSaver scheme or complying superannuation fund.
CEC rate is—

3% if the employee has been granted a rate reduction under subpart 3B or, if the employee is a member of a complying superannuation fund, the employee has a contribution rate of 3% in accordance with clause 7 of schedule 28 of the Income Tax Act 2007 and, in either case, the employer chooses to apply this rate; or

When a change occurs to the CEC rate affecting the amount of a compulsory employer contribution, the calculation of the amount of the contribution must be made at the rate applying on the day on which the salary or wages are paid.

Other contributions is the total of amounts that the employer pays or credits in relation to the employee for the period to which the payment of gross salary or wages relates, to the extent to which the amounts are—
employer contributions made in the absence of this section:
employer’s superannuation contributions made to, or amounts credited from within, (collectively, the contributions) a retirement scheme (the contributions scheme), and—

the contributions scheme was registered before 17 May 2007, or the contributions scheme is one (a succeeding scheme) for which there is, due to all relevant members transferring to the succeeding scheme by virtue of section 9BAA of the Superannuation Schemes Act 1989 or section 119G of this Act or section 179(2)﻿(b) of the Financial Markets Conduct Act 2013, a prior registered superannuation scheme (a prior scheme) and that prior scheme or another prior scheme for the contributions scheme were registered before 17 May 2007; and
the employer provided access to eligible employees to the contributions scheme or a prior scheme for the contributions scheme before 17 May 2007; and
the employee is—

employed by the employer before 1 April 2008, and the employer makes or has agreed with the employee before 1 April 2008 to make or credit the contributions to the contributions scheme or a prior scheme for the contributions scheme; or

covered by a collective agreement that is in force before 17 May 2007 and expires after 1 April 2008 under which the employer is required to make or credit the contributions to the contributions scheme or a prior scheme for the contributions scheme; or

one that has had contributions paid or credited to the contributions scheme or a prior scheme for the contributions scheme by a previous employer, and those contributions met the requirements described in paragraph (b)﻿(i) to (iii); and

the contributions scheme provides that the contributions vest completely in the employee in a period starting on or after the employee first becomes a member of the contributions scheme and ending no more than 5 years later:
employer’s superannuation contributions or superannuation subsidies in relation to an employee—
whose employment is as a member of Parliament, a judicial officer, or a constable:
who is in a class of employees prescribed in regulations made under section 230A.
Hybrid schemes amount is the amount given by subsection (7) for a retirement scheme, employer and employee described in subsection (5)﻿(b)﻿(i) to (iii)—
which do not have an amount of other contributions for the period to which the payment of gross salary or wages relates; and
for which relevant retirement benefits are calculated by adding to the employee’s total contributions a percentage of those contributions.
For the purposes of subsection (6), the amount is calculated using the following formula:
In the formula,—
member’s contribution is the amount of the employee’s contribution for the period to which the payment of gross salary or wages relates:

vesting percentage is the percentage (inclusive of amounts payable under the ESCT rules) of the employee's total contributions to be added to those contributions 5 years after the employee first becomes a member of the retirement scheme.

history.history-note: Section 101D: inserted, on 1 April 2008, by section 65 of the Taxation (KiwiSaver) Act 2007 (2007 No 110).
Section 101D(4): replaced, on 1 April 2026, by section 22(1) of the Taxation (Budget Measures) Act 2025 (2025 No 26).
Section 101D(4B): inserted, on 1 April 2018, by section 389(2) of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2017–18, Employment and Investment Income, and Remedial Matters) Act 2018 (2018 No 5).
Section 101D(5)﻿(b): amended, on 1 December 2014, by section 88(1)﻿(a) of the Financial Markets (Repeals and Amendments) Act 2013 (2013 No 70).
Section 101D(5)﻿(b)﻿(i): amended, on 1 December 2014, by section 88(1)﻿(b) of the Financial Markets (Repeals and Amendments) Act 2013 (2013 No 70).
Section 101D(5)﻿(b)﻿(i): amended, on 1 May 2011, by section 10 of the KiwiSaver Amendment Act 2011 (2011 No 8).

Section 101D(5)﻿(b)﻿(iii)﻿(B): amended (with effect on 1 April 2008), on 6 October 2009, by section 726(1) of the Taxation (International Taxation, Life Insurance, and Remedial Matters) Act 2009 (2009 No 34).

Section 101D(5)﻿(b)﻿(iii)﻿(C): added (with effect on 1 April 2008), on 6 October 2009, by section 726(1) of the Taxation (International Taxation, Life Insurance, and Remedial Matters) Act 2009 (2009 No 34).
Section 101D(5)﻿(c)﻿(i): amended, on 1 October 2008, pursuant to section 116(a)﻿(vii) of the Policing Act 2008 (2008 No 72).
Section 101D(6): amended, on 1 December 2014, by section 88(2) of the Financial Markets (Repeals and Amendments) Act 2013 (2013 No 70).
Section 101D(8)﻿(b): amended, on 1 December 2014, by section 88(3) of the Financial Markets (Repeals and Amendments) Act 2013 (2013 No 70).
Section 101D(8)﻿(b): amended (with effect on 1 April 2008), on 6 October 2009, by section 726(2) of the Taxation (International Taxation, Life Insurance, and Remedial Matters) Act 2009 (2009 No 34).

prov.label: 101E
prov.heading: Payment: allocation between schemes and funds

para.text: If the employer and employee agree to an allocation of compulsory employer contributions between an employee’s KiwiSaver scheme and complying superannuation funds, the contribution allocation agreed is used for allocating payments.
If the employer and employee cannot agree what allocation of the amount of compulsory employer contribution is for an employee’s KiwiSaver scheme or complying superannuation funds, the amount is—
first, for the employee’s KiwiSaver scheme, up to the maximum required to meet an employer’s compulsory employer contribution obligations:
second, for the employee’s complying superannuation funds, pro rata, to the extent to which an amount remains after applying paragraph (a).

history.history-note: Section 101E: inserted, on 1 April 2008, by section 65 of the Taxation (KiwiSaver) Act 2007 (2007 No 110).

prov.label: 101F
prov.heading: Payment rules: employers

para.text: If an amount of employer contribution for a payment of salary or wages is for the employee’s KiwiSaver scheme, the amount must be paid by the employer to the Commissioner. The amount is subject to the rules provided in subpart 3.

If an amount of compulsory employer contribution for a payment of salary or wages is for the employee’s complying superannuation fund, the amount must be paid by the employer to the fund’s provider no later than 1 month after the payment of salary or wages.

history.history-note: Section 101F: inserted, on 1 April 2008, by section 65 of the Taxation (KiwiSaver) Act 2007 (2007 No 110).

prov.label: 101FB
prov.heading: Grace periods: employers

para.text: An employer does not have to pay a compulsory employer contribution for a payment of gross salary or wages to an employee in a grace period described in subsection (2), if, for the whole of the relevant grace period,—
1 or more of the following apply:
the automatic enrolment rules apply to the employee:
the employer does not receive a notice under section 34(1) or 39 that the employee has opted in; and
the employer does not deduct any amount of contributions required to be deducted from an employee's salary or wages; and
the employer does not receive a notice under section 61 that requires the deduction of contributions for the employee.
The grace periods for the purposes of subsection (1) are—
the period starting on the day that the employee starts new employment and finishing on the earlier of the day—
that is 1 year after the day that the employee starts new employment:
that the employee ceases employment:
the period starting on the day that is 1 year after the day that the employee starts new employment and finishing on the earliest of—
the day that is before the day on which the employer receives a notice under section 34(1) or 39 that the employee has opted in:
the day that is before the day on which the employer receives a notice under section 61 that requires the deduction of contributions for the employee:
the day that the employee ceases employment.

history.history-note: Section 101FB: inserted (with effect on 1 April 2008), on 6 October 2009, by section 727 of the Taxation (International Taxation, Life Insurance, and Remedial Matters) Act 2009 (2009 No 34).

prov.label: 101FC
prov.heading: De minimis: other contributions and hybrid schemes amount

para.text: For a payment of gross salary or wages to an employee, an employer does not have to pay a compulsory employer contribution for the employee, if, in respect of the payment of gross salary or wages, one of the following amounts is equal to or more than the relevant CEC rate in section 101D(4):
the amount of other contributions that meets the requirements of section 101D(5)﻿(b) divided by the employee's salary or wages (as defined for the relevant retirement scheme):
the hybrid schemes amount that meets the requirements of section 101D(6) to (8) divided by the employee's salary or wages (as defined for the relevant retirement scheme).

history.history-note: Section 101FC: inserted (with effect on 1 April 2008), on 6 October 2009, by section 727 of the Taxation (International Taxation, Life Insurance, and Remedial Matters) Act 2009 (2009 No 34).
Section 101FC(a): amended, on 1 December 2014, by section 89 of the Financial Markets (Repeals and Amendments) Act 2013 (2013 No 70).
Section 101FC(b): amended, on 1 December 2014, by section 89 of the Financial Markets (Repeals and Amendments) Act 2013 (2013 No 70).

prov.label: 101G
prov.heading: Rules: providers

para.text: A provider must use the contribution allocation for a member to credit the amount of compulsory employer contribution they receive across the investment products to which a member has subscribed or has been allocated.
The contribution vests in the member immediately after it is paid to the provider, despite any provision to the contrary.

If a member of a KiwiSaver scheme will be entitled within 2 months to withdraw an amount from the fund or scheme under clause 4 of the KiwiSaver scheme rules (which relates to lock-in of funds), the provider must send a notice to the Commissioner stating the date on which the member will be entitled to withdraw. The Commissioner may notify the member’s employer of the date, for the purposes of the employer applying this subpart.

If a member of a complying superannuation fund will be entitled within 2 months to withdraw an amount from the fund under a rule the same as clause 4 of the KiwiSaver scheme rules, the provider must send a notice to the member's employer stating the date on which the member will be entitled to withdraw.

history.history-note: Section 101G: inserted, on 1 April 2008, by section 65 of the Taxation (KiwiSaver) Act 2007 (2007 No 110).
Section 101G(3): amended, on 1 July 2019, by section 316(1) of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2018–19, Modernising Tax Administration, and Remedial Matters) Act 2019 (2019 No 5).
Section 101G(3): amended, on 6 October 2009, by section 728(1)﻿(a) of the Taxation (International Taxation, Life Insurance, and Remedial Matters) Act 2009 (2009 No 34).
Section 101G(3): amended, on 6 October 2009, by section 728(1)﻿(b) of the Taxation (International Taxation, Life Insurance, and Remedial Matters) Act 2009 (2009 No 34).
Section 101G(4): amended, on 1 July 2019, by section 316(2) of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2018–19, Modernising Tax Administration, and Remedial Matters) Act 2019 (2019 No 5).
Section 101G(4): added, on 6 October 2009, by section 728(2) of the Taxation (International Taxation, Life Insurance, and Remedial Matters) Act 2009 (2009 No 34).
Heading: repealed, on 1 December 2014, by section 90 of the Financial Markets (Repeals and Amendments) Act 2013 (2013 No 70).

prov.label: 101H
prov.heading: Failure to pay: provider notice

history.history-note: Section 101H: repealed, on 1 December 2014, by section 90 of the Financial Markets (Repeals and Amendments) Act 2013 (2013 No 70).

prov.label: 101I
prov.heading: Failure to pay: FMA's duties

history.history-note: Section 101I: repealed, on 1 December 2014, by section 90 of the Financial Markets (Repeals and Amendments) Act 2013 (2013 No 70).

prov.label: 101J
prov.heading: Failure to pay: Commissioner

history.history-note: Section 101J: repealed, on 1 December 2014, by section 90 of the Financial Markets (Repeals and Amendments) Act 2013 (2013 No 70).

prov.label: 101K
prov.heading: Recovered amounts

history.history-note: Section 101K: repealed, on 1 December 2014, by section 90 of the Financial Markets (Repeals and Amendments) Act 2013 (2013 No 70).
Subpart 3B: inserted, on 1 February 2026, by section 23 of the Taxation (Budget Measures) Act 2025 (2025 No 26).
Heading: inserted, on 1 February 2026, by section 23 of the Taxation (Budget Measures) Act 2025 (2025 No 26).

prov.label: 101L
prov.heading: How to apply for rate reduction

para.text: A person to whom subpart 1 of Part 3 applies may apply to the Commissioner for a rate reduction by any means that the Commissioner accepts.
The application must include the following:
the person’s name and address; and
the person’s tax file number; and
the period for which the rate reduction is required; and
any other information the Commissioner requires.
For the purposes of subsection (2)﻿(c), the period for which the rate reduction is required must be no less than 92 days and no more than 1 year.

history.history-note: Section 101L: inserted, on 1 February 2026, by section 23 of the Taxation (Budget Measures) Act 2025 (2025 No 26).

prov.label: 101M
prov.heading: Grant of rate reduction

para.text: The Commissioner must grant a rate reduction for the period specified in the application if the application is made in accordance with section 101L.

history.history-note: Section 101M: inserted, on 1 February 2026, by section 23 of the Taxation (Budget Measures) Act 2025 (2025 No 26).

prov.label: 101N
prov.heading: Commissioner must give notice of grant of rate reduction

para.text: The Commissioner must, as soon as practicable after granting a rate reduction, give notice—
to the person who applied for the rate reduction—
that the rate reduction has been granted; and
of the date on which the rate reduction will end; and
to each employer for whom the rate reduction will apply—
that a rate reduction has been granted for the person; and
that the employer must make deductions of contributions from the salary or wages of the person at the rate set out in section 64(1)﻿(c); and
of the date on which the rate reduction will end.

history.history-note: Section 101N: inserted, on 1 February 2026, by section 23 of the Taxation (Budget Measures) Act 2025 (2025 No 26).

prov.label: 101O
prov.heading: Deductions at specified rate

para.text: If an employer is notified under section 101N for a person, the employer must make deductions of contributions from the salary or wages of the person at the rate set out in section 64(1)﻿(c) with effect from the next payment of salary or wages that the employer calculates.

history.history-note: Section 101O: inserted, on 1 February 2026, by section 23 of the Taxation (Budget Measures) Act 2025 (2025 No 26).
Heading: inserted, on 1 February 2026, by section 23 of the Taxation (Budget Measures) Act 2025 (2025 No 26).

prov.label: 101P
prov.heading: Commissioner must give notice before rate reduction ends

para.text: Before a rate reduction ends, the Commissioner must give notice of the date it ends to the person granted the rate reduction.

history.history-note: Section 101P: inserted, on 1 February 2026, by section 23 of the Taxation (Budget Measures) Act 2025 (2025 No 26).

prov.label: 101Q
prov.heading: Commissioner must give notice to employer of end of rate reduction

para.text: The Commissioner must give notice to each affected employer known to the Commissioner, as soon as practicable after the end of a rate reduction,—
of the date on which the rate reduction ended; and
that the employer must cease making deductions of contributions from the salary or wages of the person at the rate set out in section 64(1)﻿(c).

history.history-note: Section 101Q: inserted, on 1 February 2026, by section 23 of the Taxation (Budget Measures) Act 2025 (2025 No 26).

prov.label: 101R
prov.heading: Deductions at applicable rate

para.text: If an employer is notified under section 101Q about the end of a person’s rate reduction or under section 101S about the revocation of a person’s rate reduction, the employer must cease making deductions of contributions from the salary or wages of the person at the rate set out in section 64(1)﻿(c) and must start making deductions at the contribution rate that applies under section 64.
Subsection (1) applies with effect from the next payment of salary or wages that the employer calculates after the date on which the employer receives the notice referred to in that subsection.
This section is subject to any new rate reduction that is granted under this subpart.

history.history-note: Section 101R: inserted, on 1 February 2026, by section 23 of the Taxation (Budget Measures) Act 2025 (2025 No 26).
Heading: inserted, on 1 February 2026, by section 23 of the Taxation (Budget Measures) Act 2025 (2025 No 26).

prov.label: 101S
prov.heading: Revocation of rate reduction

para.text: Subject to subsection (2), a person may at any time revoke their rate reduction for an employer by giving notice to the employer requiring the employer to cease making deductions of contributions from their salary or wages at the rate set out in section 64(1)﻿(c) and to start making deductions at the applicable contribution rate under section 64(1)﻿(a), (ab), (b), or (2).
No rate reduction may be used for an employer for less than 92 days unless the employer agrees.

history.history-note: Section 101S: inserted, on 1 February 2026, by section 23 of the Taxation (Budget Measures) Act 2025 (2025 No 26).

prov.label: 101T
prov.heading: Refund if person cannot comply

para.text: This section applies if a person—
has a rate reduction that has not yet ended; and
starts new employment but cannot comply with section 22(1)﻿(c)﻿(ia).
The employer may, at any time after the person complies with section 22(1)﻿(c)﻿(ia), refund to the person the amount determined under the following formula:
The items in the formula are defined in subsections (4) and (5).
Contributions deducted is the total amount of contributions deducted by the employer from the person’s salary or wages at a contribution rate other than the rate set out in section 64(1)﻿(c).
Rate reduction amount is the amount the contributions deducted in subsection (4) would have totalled if the employer had deducted those contributions at the rate set out in section 64(1)﻿(c).
The Commissioner may refund the amount determined under subsection (2) to the person if the money is held by the Commissioner.
This section is subject to section 91B.

history.history-note: Section 101T: inserted, on 1 February 2026, by section 23 of the Taxation (Budget Measures) Act 2025 (2025 No 26).

subpart.heading: Savings suspension

history.history-note: Part 3 subpart 4 heading: replaced, on 1 April 2019, by section 321 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2018–19, Modernising Tax Administration, and Remedial Matters) Act 2019 (2019 No 5).
Heading: amended, on 1 April 2019, by section 321 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2018–19, Modernising Tax Administration, and Remedial Matters) Act 2019 (2019 No 5).

prov.heading: Who may apply for savings suspension

para.text: A person to whom subpart 1 (deductions of contributions from salary or wages) applies may apply to the Commissioner for a savings suspension—
at any time after the Commissioner receives the first contribution in respect of that person, if the person is suffering, or likely to suffer, financial hardship; or
at any time after 12 months have expired since the earlier of—
the date that the Commissioner received the first contribution in respect of that person; or
the date that a provider received the first contribution in respect of that person’s membership of a KiwiSaver scheme; or
the date that the person is first a member of a complying superannuation fund.

history.history-note: Section 102 heading: amended, on 1 April 2019, by section 321 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2018–19, Modernising Tax Administration, and Remedial Matters) Act 2019 (2019 No 5).
Section 102: amended, on 1 April 2019, by section 321 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2018–19, Modernising Tax Administration, and Remedial Matters) Act 2019 (2019 No 5).
Section 102(b)﻿(ii): amended, on 19 December 2007, by section 67 of the Taxation (KiwiSaver) Act 2007 (2007 No 110).
Section 102(b)﻿(iii): added, on 19 December 2007, by section 67 of the Taxation (KiwiSaver) Act 2007 (2007 No 110).

prov.heading: How to apply for savings suspension

para.text: An application for a savings suspension may be made by any means that the Commissioner accepts.
The application must tell the Commissioner—
the person’s name and address; and
the person’s tax file number; and
the name and address of each of the person’s employers to whom the person intends that the suspension will apply, if required by the Commissioner; and
the period of time for which the suspension is required; and
in the case of an application made under section 102(a), details of the financial hardship; and
any other information that the Commissioner requires.

history.history-note: Section 103 heading: amended, on 1 April 2019, by section 321 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2018–19, Modernising Tax Administration, and Remedial Matters) Act 2019 (2019 No 5).
Section 103(1): amended, on 1 April 2019, by section 321 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2018–19, Modernising Tax Administration, and Remedial Matters) Act 2019 (2019 No 5).
Section 103(2)﻿(c): replaced, on 1 April 2020, by section 40 of the Taxation (KiwiSaver, Student Loans, and Remedial Matters) Act 2020 (2020 No 5).
Section 103(2)﻿(d): amended, on 1 April 2019, by section 321 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2018–19, Modernising Tax Administration, and Remedial Matters) Act 2019 (2019 No 5).

prov.heading: Granting of savings suspension

para.text: The Commissioner must accept an application for a savings suspension, and grant a savings suspension, if the Commissioner is satisfied that the person meets the requirements of section 102, and the application is made in accordance with section 103.
A savings suspension granted in respect of an application made under section 102(a) must be granted for a period of 92 days, unless the Commissioner agrees to a longer period.
A savings suspension granted in respect of an application made under section 102(b) must be granted for—
a minimum period of 92 days; and
a maximum period of whichever is the shorter of—
1 year; or
the period specified in the application.

history.history-note: Section 104 heading: amended, on 1 April 2019, by section 321 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2018–19, Modernising Tax Administration, and Remedial Matters) Act 2019 (2019 No 5).
Section 104(1): amended, on 1 April 2019, by section 321 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2018–19, Modernising Tax Administration, and Remedial Matters) Act 2019 (2019 No 5).
Section 104(2): amended, on 1 April 2020, by section 41(1) of the Taxation (KiwiSaver, Student Loans, and Remedial Matters) Act 2020 (2020 No 5).
Section 104(2): amended, on 1 April 2019, by section 321 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2018–19, Modernising Tax Administration, and Remedial Matters) Act 2019 (2019 No 5).
Section 104(3): amended, on 1 April 2019, by section 321 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2018–19, Modernising Tax Administration, and Remedial Matters) Act 2019 (2019 No 5).
Section 104(3)﻿(a): amended, on 1 April 2020, by section 41(2) of the Taxation (KiwiSaver, Student Loans, and Remedial Matters) Act 2020 (2020 No 5).
Section 104(3)﻿(b)﻿(i): replaced, on 1 April 2019, by section 317 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2018–19, Modernising Tax Administration, and Remedial Matters) Act 2019 (2019 No 5).

prov.heading: Commissioner must give notice of grant of savings suspension

para.text: The Commissioner must, as soon as practicable after granting a savings suspension, give notice—
to the person who applied for the suspension—
that the suspension has been granted; and
of the date on which the suspension will end; and
to each relevant employer—
that a savings suspension has been granted in respect of the person; and
that the employer must stop making deductions of contributions from the salary or wages of the person; and
to the provider of the person’s KiwiSaver scheme—
that a savings suspension has been granted in respect of the person; and
of the names of the relevant employers; and
that deductions of contributions may not be made from the salary or wages paid to the person by the relevant employers during the period of the suspension.

In this section, relevant employer, for an application for a savings suspension that was required by the Commissioner to contain the information described in section 103(2)﻿(c), means each employer to whom the person stated, in the application, that the savings suspension was intended to apply.

history.history-note: Section 105 heading: amended, on 1 April 2019, by section 321 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2018–19, Modernising Tax Administration, and Remedial Matters) Act 2019 (2019 No 5).
Section 105(1): amended, on 1 April 2019, by section 321 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2018–19, Modernising Tax Administration, and Remedial Matters) Act 2019 (2019 No 5).
Section 105(1)﻿(a): amended, on 1 April 2019, by section 321 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2018–19, Modernising Tax Administration, and Remedial Matters) Act 2019 (2019 No 5).
Section 105(1)﻿(a)﻿(i): amended, on 1 April 2019, by section 321 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2018–19, Modernising Tax Administration, and Remedial Matters) Act 2019 (2019 No 5).
Section 105(1)﻿(a)﻿(ii): amended, on 1 April 2019, by section 321 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2018–19, Modernising Tax Administration, and Remedial Matters) Act 2019 (2019 No 5).
Section 105(1)﻿(b)﻿(i): amended, on 1 April 2019, by section 321 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2018–19, Modernising Tax Administration, and Remedial Matters) Act 2019 (2019 No 5).
Section 105(1)﻿(c)﻿(i): amended, on 1 April 2019, by section 321 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2018–19, Modernising Tax Administration, and Remedial Matters) Act 2019 (2019 No 5).
Section 105(1)﻿(c)﻿(iii): amended, on 1 April 2019, by section 321 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2018–19, Modernising Tax Administration, and Remedial Matters) Act 2019 (2019 No 5).
Section 105(2): replaced, on 1 April 2020, by section 42 of the Taxation (KiwiSaver, Student Loans, and Remedial Matters) Act 2020 (2020 No 5).

prov.heading: When deductions stop at start of savings suspension

para.text: If an employer is notified under section 105, subpart 1 ceases to apply—
to the employer in respect of the person who applied for the savings suspension; and
with effect on the next payment of salary or wages that the employer calculates after the date on which the employer receives the notice.

history.history-note: Section 106 heading: amended, on 1 April 2019, by section 321 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2018–19, Modernising Tax Administration, and Remedial Matters) Act 2019 (2019 No 5).
Section 106(a): amended, on 1 April 2019, by section 321 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2018–19, Modernising Tax Administration, and Remedial Matters) Act 2019 (2019 No 5).

prov.heading: Employers to whom savings suspension applies

para.text: A savings suspension granted under this subpart, while it is in force,—

has effect, subject to section 108, in respect of each employer to whom the person stated, in the application for the savings suspension, that the savings suspension was intended to apply, if the application was required by the Commissioner to contain the information described in section 103(2)﻿(c); and
may be used, if the person chooses, in respect of any other employer.

history.history-note: Section 107 heading: amended, on 1 April 2019, by section 321 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2018–19, Modernising Tax Administration, and Remedial Matters) Act 2019 (2019 No 5).
Section 107: amended, on 1 April 2019, by section 321 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2018–19, Modernising Tax Administration, and Remedial Matters) Act 2019 (2019 No 5).
Section 107(a): amended, on 1 April 2020, by section 43(1) (and see section 43(2) for application) of the Taxation (KiwiSaver, Student Loans, and Remedial Matters) Act 2020 (2020 No 5).
Section 107(a): amended, on 1 April 2019, by section 321 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2018–19, Modernising Tax Administration, and Remedial Matters) Act 2019 (2019 No 5).

prov.heading: Savings suspensions have 92-day minimum life

para.text: The purpose of this section is to prevent employees requesting employers to stop and start deductions of contributions too often.
No savings suspension may be used in respect of an employer for less than 92 days unless the employer agrees.

history.history-note: Section 108 heading: amended, on 1 April 2020, by section 44(1) of the Taxation (KiwiSaver, Student Loans, and Remedial Matters) Act 2020 (2020 No 5).
Section 108 heading: amended, on 1 April 2019, by section 321 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2018–19, Modernising Tax Administration, and Remedial Matters) Act 2019 (2019 No 5).
Section 108(2): amended, on 1 April 2020, by section 44(2) of the Taxation (KiwiSaver, Student Loans, and Remedial Matters) Act 2020 (2020 No 5).
Section 108(2): amended, on 1 April 2019, by section 321 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2018–19, Modernising Tax Administration, and Remedial Matters) Act 2019 (2019 No 5).
Heading: amended, on 1 April 2019, by section 321 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2018–19, Modernising Tax Administration, and Remedial Matters) Act 2019 (2019 No 5).

prov.heading: Commissioner must give notice before savings suspensions ends

para.text: The Commissioner must give notice to a person who is on a savings suspension before the suspension ends.

history.history-note: Section 109 heading: amended, on 1 April 2019, by section 321 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2018–19, Modernising Tax Administration, and Remedial Matters) Act 2019 (2019 No 5).
Section 109: amended, on 1 April 2019, by section 321 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2018–19, Modernising Tax Administration, and Remedial Matters) Act 2019 (2019 No 5).

prov.heading: Commissioner must give notice to employer of end of savings suspension

para.text: The Commissioner must give notice to each affected employer known to the Commissioner, as soon as practicable after the end of a savings suspension,—
of the date on which the savings suspension ended; and
that the employer must start making deductions of contributions from the salary or wages of the person.

history.history-note: Section 110 heading: amended, on 1 April 2019, by section 321 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2018–19, Modernising Tax Administration, and Remedial Matters) Act 2019 (2019 No 5).
Section 110: amended, on 1 April 2019, by section 321 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2018–19, Modernising Tax Administration, and Remedial Matters) Act 2019 (2019 No 5).
Section 110(a): amended, on 1 April 2019, by section 321 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2018–19, Modernising Tax Administration, and Remedial Matters) Act 2019 (2019 No 5).

prov.heading: When deductions start at end of savings suspension

para.text: If an employer is notified under section 110 about the end of a person’s savings suspension, or under section 112 about the revocation of a person’s savings suspension, subpart 1 applies—
to the employer in respect of that person; and
with effect on the next payment of salary or wages that the employer calculates after the date on which the employer receives the notice.
This section is subject to any new savings suspension that is granted under this subpart.

history.history-note: Section 111 heading: amended, on 1 April 2019, by section 321 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2018–19, Modernising Tax Administration, and Remedial Matters) Act 2019 (2019 No 5).
Section 111(1): amended, on 1 April 2019, by section 321 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2018–19, Modernising Tax Administration, and Remedial Matters) Act 2019 (2019 No 5).
Section 111(2): amended, on 1 April 2019, by section 321 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2018–19, Modernising Tax Administration, and Remedial Matters) Act 2019 (2019 No 5).

subpart.crosshead: Revocation and reinstatement of savings suspension

history.history-note: Heading: amended, on 1 April 2019, by section 321 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2018–19, Modernising Tax Administration, and Remedial Matters) Act 2019 (2019 No 5).

prov.heading: Revocation and reinstatement of savings suspension

para.text: Subject to section 108, a person may at any time revoke his or her savings suspension in respect of an employer by giving notice to the employer requiring the employer to start making deductions from salary or wages under subpart 1.

A person may at any time reinstate his or her savings suspension in respect of an employer by giving notice to the employer requiring the employer to stop making deductions from salary or wages under subpart 1.
Sections 106 and 108 apply, with necessary modifications, as if the reinstatement of a savings suspension were the granting of a savings suspension.

history.history-note: Section 112 heading: amended, on 1 April 2019, by section 321 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2018–19, Modernising Tax Administration, and Remedial Matters) Act 2019 (2019 No 5).
Section 112(1): amended, on 1 April 2019, by section 321 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2018–19, Modernising Tax Administration, and Remedial Matters) Act 2019 (2019 No 5).
Section 112(2): amended, on 1 April 2019, by section 321 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2018–19, Modernising Tax Administration, and Remedial Matters) Act 2019 (2019 No 5).
Section 112(3): amended, on 1 April 2019, by section 321 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2018–19, Modernising Tax Administration, and Remedial Matters) Act 2019 (2019 No 5).

prov.label: 112B
prov.heading: Non-deduction notices

para.text: A person who may withdraw an amount from a fund or scheme under clause 4 of the KiwiSaver rules or a rule the same as that clause may give to their employer a notice (a non-deduction notice) stating that the employer must stop making deductions of contributions from the person's salary or wages under subpart 1.

The non-deduction notice is valid, for the purposes of section 62(bb), for the first payment of salary or wages after the non-deduction notice is given to the person's employer. It is valid for subsequent payments of salary or wages, until the person revokes that non-deduction notice by giving to the employer another notice (a revocation notice), in accordance with subsection (3), stating that the employer must start making deductions of contributions from the person's salary or wages under subpart 1.
A person must not give a revocation notice to the employer within 92 days of giving a non-deduction notice to them, unless the employer agrees to receive the revocation notice within those 92 days.

history.history-note: Section 112B: inserted (with effect on 1 July 2012), on 2 November 2012, by section 235 of the Taxation (Annual Rates, Returns Filing, and Remedial Matters) Act 2012 (2012 No 88).
Section 112B(1): amended, on 1 July 2019, by section 318 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2018–19, Modernising Tax Administration, and Remedial Matters) Act 2019 (2019 No 5).
Section 112B(3): amended, on 1 April 2020, by section 45 of the Taxation (KiwiSaver, Student Loans, and Remedial Matters) Act 2020 (2020 No 5).

prov.heading: Refund of initial contributions

para.text: A person may apply to the Commissioner for a refund of any contributions that are being held in the holding account under section 75 if the person is suffering, or likely to suffer, significant financial hardship or is suffering serious illness.
In this section, significant financial hardship has the same meaning as in clause 11 of the KiwiSaver rules and serious illness has the same meaning as in clause 12 of those rules.
The application may be made by any means that the Commissioner accepts.
The application must tell the Commissioner—
the person’s name and address; and
the person’s tax file number; and
details of the significant financial hardship or serious illness; and
any other information that the Commissioner requires.
The Commissioner must refund the contributions to which the application relates, if the Commissioner is reasonably satisfied that—
the person and the application meet the requirements of this section; and
reasonable alternative sources of funding have been explored and have been exhausted.
However, the Commissioner—
must not refund under this section any employer contributions that were made under section 93; and

may direct that, despite subsection (5), the amount to be refunded under this section is limited to a specified amount that, in the Commissioner’s opinion, is required to alleviate the particular hardship.
The Commissioner must give notice of the refund to the provider of the relevant KiwiSaver scheme (if any).

history.history-note: Section 113(5): substituted, on 19 December 2007, by section 68 of the Taxation (KiwiSaver) Act 2007 (2007 No 110).
Section 113(6): substituted, on 19 December 2007, by section 68 of the Taxation (KiwiSaver) Act 2007 (2007 No 110).

prov.heading: Refunds if employee loses, etc, savings suspension notice

para.text: This section applies if—
an employee has a savings suspension that has not yet ended; and
the employee starts new employment, but cannot comply with section 22(1)﻿(c)﻿(ii).

The employer may, at any time after the employee complies with section 22(1)﻿(c)﻿(ii), refund to the employee any contributions that were deducted from the employee’s salary or wages before the employee complied.
The Commissioner may refund that money to the employee if the money is held by the Commissioner.
This section is subject to section 91B.

Section 114(1)﻿(a): amended, on 1 April 2019, by section 321 of the Taxation (Annual Rates for 2018–19, Modernising Tax Administration, and Remedial Matters) Act 2019 (2019 No 5).

Part 4: replaced, on 1 December 2014, by section 92 of the Financial Markets (Repeals and Amendments) Act 2013 (2013 No 70).

prov.heading: Overview of Part

para.text: This Part contains governance provisions relating to schemes that are additional to those contained in the Financial Markets Conduct Act 2013, and other matters related to the functions of the Financial Markets Authority, as follows:
subpart 1 provides for implied terms in trust deeds and regulates other scheme matters:
subpart 2 provides for default KiwiSaver schemes:
subpart 3 provides for certain schemes to continue to be complying superannuation funds:
subpart 4 provides for certain employers to continue to be exempt employers, with the effect that the automatic enrolment rules will not apply to their employees when they start new employment:
subpart 5 contains miscellaneous matters.

history.history-note: Section 115: replaced, on 1 December 2014, by section 92 of the Financial Markets (Repeals and Amendments) Act 2013 (2013 No 70).

subpart.heading: Implied terms and other regulation of schemes

history.history-note: Subpart 1: replaced, on 1 December 2014, by section 92 of the Financial Markets (Repeals and Amendments) Act 2013 (2013 No 70).
Heading: inserted, on 1 December 2014, by section 92 of the Financial Markets (Repeals and Amendments) Act 2013 (2013 No 70).

prov.heading: KiwiSaver scheme rules are implied in trust deeds establishing KiwiSaver scheme

para.text: The provisions set out in Schedule 1 are to be known as the KiwiSaver scheme rules.
The KiwiSaver scheme rules set out in Schedule 1 are implied in every trust deed that establishes a KiwiSaver scheme in relation to the KiwiSaver scheme.
The KiwiSaver scheme rules—
apply despite anything to the contrary in the trust deed; and
are enforceable by the manager, the supervisor, or any member of the scheme.

subpart.crosshead: Provisions about unreasonable fees that apply to both KiwiSaver schemes and complying superannuation funds

history.history-note: Heading: inserted, on 1 December 2014, by section 92 of the Financial Markets (Repeals and Amendments) Act 2013 (2013 No 70).

prov.heading: Duty to give notice to FMA about fee increases

para.text: Any person who increases a fee must give notice of the increase to the FMA before or as soon as is reasonably practicable after the increase takes effect if—
the person is referred to in clause 2 of the KiwiSaver scheme rules (which relates to a requirement that fees not be unreasonable), and that clause applies to the fee; or

(in the case of a complying superannuation fund) the person would be referred to in clause 2 of the KiwiSaver scheme rules, and that clause would apply to the fee, if the complying superannuation fund were a KiwiSaver scheme.

Every person commits an offence, and is liable on conviction to a fine not exceeding the amount set out in subsection (4), who refuses or fails without reasonable excuse to give notice to the FMA of a fee increase as and when required by this section.
If any body corporate commits an offence under this section, every officer of the body corporate who knowingly authorises or permits the offence also commits the offence.
A person who is convicted of an offence under this section is liable,—
the first time that the person is convicted, to a fine not exceeding $25,000; and
on every other occasion that the person is convicted, to a fine not exceeding $50,000.

prov.cf: 1989 No 10 s 39; 2006 No 40 ss 189B, 199 as at October 2011

history.history-note: Section 117: replaced, on 1 December 2014, by section 92 of the Financial Markets (Repeals and Amendments) Act 2013 (2013 No 70).

prov.label: 117A
prov.heading: Restrictions on transactions

history.history-note: Section 117A: repealed, on 1 December 2014, by section 92 of the Financial Markets (Repeals and Amendments) Act 2013 (2013 No 70).

prov.heading: Exercise of functions by FMA about unreasonable fees

para.text: In considering whether a fee is unreasonable in relation to the provision of a KiwiSaver scheme or a complying superannuation fund, the FMA—
must have regard to any prescribed matter; and
may have regard to any other matter that the FMA considers relevant; and
may make decisions in accordance with any prescribed process.

history.history-note: Section 118: replaced, on 1 December 2014, by section 92 of the Financial Markets (Repeals and Amendments) Act 2013 (2013 No 70).

prov.heading: Powers of High Court in relation to unreasonable fees

para.text: If the High Court is satisfied, on the application of a member of a scheme or the FMA, that any of the persons referred to in section 117(1)﻿(a) or (b) has charged a fee that is unreasonable, it may order that the fee be annulled or reduced.
The High Court may make any other order it thinks fit for the purpose of giving effect to an order under subsection (1).
An application for an order may be made within 1 year of the day that the fee is imposed or debited.
In determining whether a fee is unreasonable for the purposes of this section, the High Court—
must have regard to any prescribed matter; and
may have regard to any other matter it thinks fit.

prov.cf: 1989 No 10 s 40; 2006 No 40 s 189C as at October 2011

prov.heading: Terms relating to backdated validation implied into trust deeds of KiwiSaver schemes

para.text: Terms necessary for giving effect to the law relating to back-dated validation of invalid membership under subpart 4 of Part 2 are implied into the trust deed that establishes a KiwiSaver scheme in relation to the KiwiSaver scheme.
The terms—
apply despite anything to the contrary in the trust deed of the scheme; and
are enforceable by the manager, the supervisor, or a member of the scheme.

history.history-note: Section 120: replaced, on 1 December 2014, by section 92 of the Financial Markets (Repeals and Amendments) Act 2013 (2013 No 70).

prov.heading: Terms relating to members’ tax credits implied into trust deeds of KiwiSaver schemes and complying superannuation funds

para.text: Terms necessary for giving effect to the law relating to the tax credits described in section MK 1 of the Income Tax Act 2007 are implied into the trust deed that establishes—
a KiwiSaver scheme in relation to the KiwiSaver scheme:
a complying superannuation fund in relation to the complying superannuation fund.
The terms—
apply despite anything to the contrary in the trust deed of the scheme; and
are enforceable by the manager, the supervisor, or a member of the scheme.

history.history-note: Section 121: replaced, on 1 December 2014, by section 92 of the Financial Markets (Repeals and Amendments) Act 2013 (2013 No 70).

prov.heading: Terms relating to compulsory employer contributions implied into trust deeds of KiwiSaver schemes and complying superannuation funds

para.text: Terms necessary for giving effect to the law relating to compulsory employer contributions are implied into the trust deed that establishes—
a KiwiSaver scheme in relation to the KiwiSaver scheme:
a complying superannuation fund in relation to the complying superannuation fund.
The terms—
apply despite anything to the contrary in the trust deed of the scheme; and
are enforceable by the manager, the supervisor, or a member of the scheme.

prov.heading: Terms relating to lump sum payments by complying superannuation funds

para.text: Terms necessary for giving effect to clause 2(c) of Schedule 28 of the Income Tax Act 2007 are implied into the trust deed that establishes a complying superannuation fund in relation to the complying superannuation fund.
The terms—
apply despite anything to the contrary in the trust deed of the scheme; and
are enforceable by the manager, the supervisor, or a member of the scheme.

history.history-note: Section 123: replaced, on 1 December 2014, by section 92 of the Financial Markets (Repeals and Amendments) Act 2013 (2013 No 70).
Heading: inserted, on 1 December 2014, by section 92 of the Financial Markets (Repeals and Amendments) Act 2013 (2013 No 70).

prov.heading: Requirement for annual return

para.text: The manager of a KiwiSaver scheme and the manager of a complying superannuation fund must provide an annual return to the FMA that—
is in the prescribed form; and
meets any further prescribed requirements.
The prescribed requirements may include a requirement to provide statistical information in relation to the KiwiSaver scheme or complying superannuation fund.
The annual return must be provided before the prescribed date and relate to the prescribed 12-month period.
Nothing in this section requires the manager to provide—
information about an identifiable individual; or
information that is not in the possession or control of the manager; or
information that is not reasonably ascertainable from information that is in the possession or control of the manager.

Every person commits an offence, and is liable on conviction to a fine not exceeding the amount set out in subsection (7), who refuses or fails without reasonable excuse to provide an annual return under this section as and when required by this Part or any regulations made under section 228.
If a body corporate commits an offence under this section, every officer of the body corporate who knowingly authorises or permits the offence also commits the offence.
A person who is convicted of an offence under this section is liable,—
the first time that the person is convicted, to a fine not exceeding $25,000; and
on every other occasion that the person is convicted, to a fine not exceeding $50,000.

history.history-note: Section 124: replaced, on 1 December 2014, by section 92 of the Financial Markets (Repeals and Amendments) Act 2013 (2013 No 70).

prov.heading: Unclaimed money held in KiwiSaver scheme

para.text: Section 151 of the Trusts Act 2019 applies subject to this section in relation to a member’s interest in a KiwiSaver scheme held by, or in the control of, the supervisor or manager of that scheme.
Section 151 of the Trusts Act 2019 applies to the member’s interest only if, at the time that section is applied,—

the member of the scheme in respect of which the trust exists is able to be identified, according to the information held by or available to the manager, as being at least 5 years older than the date on which a withdrawal is permitted under clause 4 of the KiwiSaver scheme rules; and
the manager has ensured that reasonable efforts have been made to locate the member but the member is unable to be found; and
there has been no contribution made to the member’s account in the preceding 5 years, excluding any Crown contribution.
